# Datasource datetime offset on timeline (2026-01-23 21:10Z)

_**User**_

@pypho_timeline All the datasources should be loaded from .xdf file, and have real datetime objects. Why do they all seem to internally have some sort of relative offset (and are displayed on the timeline with wrong datetimes)?

---

_**Agent (model default, mode Agent)**_


Investigating why datasources from .xdf files show incorrect datetimes. Checking how .xdf files are loaded and how datetimes are handled.

        "contents": "def get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header: dict) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n\n    \n    for path in possible_paths:\n        try:\n            value = file_header\n            for key in path:\n                if isinstance(value, dict) and key in value:\n                    value = value[key]\n                    # XDF headers often have values as lists with single element\n                    if isinstance(value, list) and len(value) &gt; 0:\n                        value = value[0]\n                else:\n                    value = None\n                    break\n            \n            if value is not None:\n                # Try to parse as datetime\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)):\n                    # Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch)\n                    try:\n                        dt = datetime.fromtimestamp(value, tz=timezone.utc)\n                        return dt\n                    except (ValueError, OSError):\n                        # Try as milliseconds if seconds fails\n                        try:\n                            dt = datetime.fromtimestamp(value / 1000.0, tz=timezone.utc)\n                            return dt\n                        except (ValueError, OSError):\n                            logger.warning(f\"Could not parse timestamp value {value} as Unix timestamp\")\n                            continue\n                elif isinstance(value, str):\n                    # Try ISO format or other string formats\n                    try",

        "contents": "def get_earliest_reference_datetime(file_headers: list, datasources: list) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n    \"\"\"Get the earliest reference datetime from multiple sources.\n    \n    This function tries to extract reference datetimes from XDF file headers,\n    and falls back to using the earliest timestamp from datasources if headers\n    don't contain recording start times.\n    \n    Args:\n        file_headers: List of XDF file header dictionaries\n        datasources: List of TrackDatasource instances (for fallback)\n        \n    Returns:\n        datetime object, or None if no reference can be determined\n    \"\"\"\n    reference_datetimes = []\n    \n    # Try to get from XDF headers\n    for file_header in file_headers:\n        ref_dt = get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header)\n        if ref_dt is not None:\n            reference_datetimes.append(ref_dt)\n    \n    # If we found any from headers, use the earliest one\n    if reference_datetimes:\n        return min(reference_datetimes)\n    \n    # Fallback: use earliest timestamp from datasources as Unix epoch reference\n    # This assumes timestamps are relative to Unix epoch\n    if datasources:\n        earliest_timestamp = min([ds.total_df_start_end_times[0] for ds in datasources if ds is not None])\n        # Use Unix epoch (1970-01-01 UTC) as reference (timezone-aware)\n        return UNIX_EPOCH_UTC\n    \n    # Last resort: return Unix epoch (timezone-aware, UTC)\n    logger.warning(\"No reference datetime found, using Unix epoch (1970-01-01 UTC)\")\n    return UNIX_EPOCH_UTC",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.cursor\\plans\\convert_time_axes_to_datetime_1b6dd680.plan.md",
        "range": {

        },
        "contents": "---\nname: Convert time axes to datetime\noverview: Convert all x (time) axes for timeline tracks from float seconds to datetime axes, using XDF file header recording start time as the reference datetime when available.\ntodos: []\n---\n\n# Convert Time Axes to Datetime Axes\n\n## Overview\n\nConvert all x (time) axes for timeline tracks from float seconds to datetime axes. **Critical requirement**: All tracks must use a single, consistent absolute datetime reference to maintain real-time alignment, even when tracks start/stop independently. The reference datetime will be extracted from XDF file headers when available, with fallback options for other data sources.\n\n## Key Changes\n\n### 1. Add Datetime Reference Management (CRITICAL: Single Reference for Alignment)\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/timeline_builder.py&#96;\n  - Extract reference datetime from XDF file headers in &#96;build_from_xdf_files()&#96;\n  - **Use a single reference datetime for the entire timeline** - this ensures all tracks align in real time\n  - For multiple XDF files: Use the earliest recording start time as the common reference\n  - Store reference datetime in &#96;TimelineBuilder&#96; instance and pass to &#96;SimpleTimelineWidget&#96;\n  - All tracks in a timeline must use the same reference datetime for proper alignment\n  - Handle fallback when XDF header doesn't have recording start time (use Unix epoch or earliest timestamp across all datasources)\n\n### 2. Update SimpleTimelineWidget\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/widgets/simple_timeline_widget.py&#96;\n  - Add &#96;reference_datetime&#96; parameter to &#96;__init__()&#96;\n  - Store reference datetime as instance attribute\n  - Convert time values when setting ranges and labels\n\n### 3. Configure PyQtGraph DateAxisItem\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/core/pyqtgraph_time_synchronized_widget.py&#96;\n  - Replace standard bottom axis with &#96;pg.DateAxisItem&#96; in &#96;_buildGraphics()&#96;",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "\"\"\"Helper functions for datetime conversion and XDF header parsing.\n\nThis module provides utilities for converting between float timestamps and datetime objects,\nand extracting reference datetimes from XDF file headers.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone\nfrom typing import Optional\nimport logging\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n# Unix epoch in UTC (timezone-aware)\nUNIX_EPOCH_UTC = datetime(1970, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n\n\ndef create_am_pm_date_axis(orientation='bottom'):\n    \"\"\"Create a DateAxisItem with 12-hour AM/PM time format.\n    \n    Args:\n        orientation: Axis orientation ('bottom', 'top', 'left', 'right')\n        \n    Returns:\n        Custom DateAxisItem instance with AM/PM formatting, or None if DateAxisItem is not available\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        import pyphoplacecellanalysis.External.pyqtgraph as pg",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"Helper functions for datetime conversion and XDF header parsing.",

            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "text": "This module provides utilities for converting between float timestamps and datetime objects,",
            "lineNumber": 3

            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "text": "\"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 5
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "text": "from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone",
            "lineNumber": 7
          },
          {
            "text": "from typing import Optional",
            "lineNumber": 8
          },
          {
            "text": "import logging",
            "lineNumber": 9
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "text": "logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)",
            "lineNumber": 11
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },

            "text": "# Unix epoch in UTC (timezone-aware)",
            "lineNumber": 13
          },
          {
            "text": "UNIX_EPOCH_UTC = datetime(1970, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)",

          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16
          },
          {
            "text": "def create_am_pm_date_axis(orientation='bottom'):",

          },
          {
            "text": "        ",
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "text": "    Returns:",
            "lineNumber": 23
          },
          {
            "text": "        Custom DateAxisItem instance with AM/PM formatting, or None if DateAxisItem is not available",
            "lineNumber": 24

          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 25
          },
          {
            "text": "    try:",
            "lineNumber": 26

            "text": "        import pyphoplacecellanalysis.External.pyqtgraph as pg",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.cursor\\plans\\convert_time_axes_to_datetime_1b6dd680.plan.md",

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 92,
            "column": 53
          }
        },
        "contents": "- **Maintain backward compatibility**: Internal calculations can still use float seconds (relative to reference), but display uses datetime\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### PyQtGraph DateAxisItem Usage\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nfrom pyphoplacecellanalysis.External.pyqtgraph import DateAxisItem\naxis = DateAxisItem(orientation='bottom')\nplot_item.setAxisItems({'bottom': axis})\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Time Conversion Pattern (Ensuring Alignment)\n\n- Store reference datetime as &#96;datetime.datetime&#96; object (single instance shared across all tracks)\n- Convert float timestamps to datetime: &#96;reference_datetime + timedelta(seconds=timestamp)&#96;\n- **All tracks use the same reference_datetime** - this ensures real-time alignment\n- Use datetime values for axis ranges and labels (display layer)\n- Keep internal calculations in float seconds for performance (data layer)\n- When tracks start at different times, their timestamps are converted using the same reference, so they appear at correct absolute positions on the datetime axis\n\n### XDF Header Parsing\n\n- Check &#96;file_header&#96; for recording start time\n- Common locations: &#96;file_header['info']['recording']['start_time']&#96; or similar\n- Parse ISO format or Unix timestamp format as needed",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "- **Maintain backward compatibility**: Internal calculations can still use float seconds (relative to reference), but display uses datetime",
            "lineNumber": 68

            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71

            "text": "### PyQtGraph DateAxisItem Usage",
            "lineNumber": 72

          {
            "lineNumber": 73
          },

            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;python",
            "lineNumber": 74

          {
            "text": "from pyphoplacecellanalysis.External.pyqtgraph import DateAxisItem",
            "lineNumber": 75

          {
            "text": "axis = DateAxisItem(orientation='bottom')",
            "lineNumber": 76

            "lineNumber": 77

        "contents": "- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/core/pyqtgraph_time_synchronized_widget.py&#96;\n  - Replace standard bottom axis with &#96;pg.DateAxisItem&#96; in &#96;_buildGraphics()&#96;\n  - Update &#96;on_window_changed()&#96; to convert float times to datetime before setting ranges\n  - Ensure all time operations use datetime-aware values\n\n### 4. Update Timeline Builder Track Creation\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/timeline_builder.py&#96;\n  - In &#96;_add_tracks_to_timeline()&#96;, configure axes with datetime formatting\n  - Convert &#96;setXRange()&#96; calls to use datetime values\n  - Update &#96;setLabel()&#96; to use datetime format instead of 's' units\n\n### 5. Update Other Axis Configuration Points\n\n- **Files to update**:\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/widgets/simple_timeline_widget.py&#96; - &#96;add_video_track()&#96; method\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/__main__.py&#96; - Main entry point axis configuration\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/docking/specific_dock_widget_mixin.py&#96; - Overview plot axes\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/docking/dynamic_dock_display_area.py&#96; - Widget axis configuration\n\n### 6. Helper Functions\n\n- **New file**: &#96;pypho_timeline/utils/datetime_helpers.py&#96;\n  - &#96;get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header)&#96; - Extract datetime from XDF header\n  - &#96;float_to_datetime(timestamp, reference_datetime)&#96; - Convert float to datetime\n  - &#96;datetime_to_float(dt, reference_datetime)&#96; - Convert datetime back to float (for internal calculations)\n\n### 7. Handle Edge Cases (Maintaining Real-Time Alignment)\n\n- **Multiple XDF files**: Use the earliest reference datetime across all files as the common reference point\n- **Mixed data sources**: When combining XDF and video tracks, use the earliest available reference datetime\n- **Video-only timelines**: Use Unix epoch or configurable reference (must be consistent across all video tracks)\n- **Missing XDF header info**: Fallback to Unix epoch (1970-01-01) or use earliest timestamp across all datasources\n- **Track start/stop independence**: Tracks can have different start/stop times, but all use the same reference datetime for alignment\n- **Maintain backward compatibility**: Internal calculations can still use float seconds (relative to reference), but display uses datetime\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### PyQtGraph DateAxisItem Usage\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python",

          {
            "text": "- **Missing XDF header info**: Fallback to Unix epoch (1970-01-01) or use earliest timestamp across all datasources",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Track start/stop independence**: Tracks can have different start/stop times, but all use the same reference datetime for alignment",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Maintain backward compatibility**: Internal calculations can still use float seconds (relative to reference), but display uses datetime",
            "lineNumber": 68

        "contents": "def float_to_datetime(timestamp: float, reference_datetime: datetime) -&gt; datetime:\n    \"\"\"Convert float timestamp to datetime using reference datetime.\n    \n    Args:\n        timestamp: Float timestamp in seconds (relative to reference)\n        reference_datetime: Reference datetime object\n        \n    Returns:\n        datetime object representing absolute time (timezone-aware, UTC)\n    \"\"\"\n    if reference_datetime is None:\n        raise ValueError(\"reference_datetime cannot be None\")\n    \n    # Make reference_datetime timezone-aware if it's naive\n    if reference_datetime.tzinfo is None:\n        # Assume UTC if naive\n        reference_datetime = reference_datetime.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n    \n    result = reference_datetime + timedelta(seconds=timestamp)\n    return result\n\n\ndef datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt: datetime) -&gt; float:\n    \"\"\"",

Optional[SimpleTimelineWidget]:\n        \"\"\"Build a timeline widget from multiple XDF files, merging streams by name.\n        \n        Streams with the same name across different files will be merged into a single track.\n        Timestamps are preserved as absolute values (no time shifting).\n        \n        Args:\n            xdf_file_paths: List of paths to XDF files\n            window_duration: Duration of the time window (default: auto-calculated from data)\n            window_start_time: Start time of the window (default: auto-calculated from data)\n            add_example_tracks: Whether to add example tracks (default: False)\n            window_title: Custom window title (default: auto-generated from filenames)\n            window_size: Window size as (width, height) tuple (default: (1000, 800))\n            \n        Returns:\n            SimpleTimelineWidget instance, or None if no streams found\n            \n        Example:\n            builder = TimelineBuilder()\n            timeline = builder.build_from_xdf_files([\n                Path(\"file1.xdf\"),\n                Path(\"file2.xdf\")\n            ])\n        \"\"\"\n        if not xdf_file_paths:\n            raise ValueError(\"xdf_file_paths list cannot be empty\")\n        \n        print(\"=\" * 60)\n        if len(xdf_file_paths) == 1:\n            print(f\"pyPhoTimeline - Load all modalities from XDF: {xdf_file_paths[0]}\")\n        else:\n            print(f\"pyPhoTimeline - Load all modalities from {len(xdf_file_paths)} XDF files:\")\n            for path in xdf_file_paths:\n                print(f\"  - {path}\")\n        print(\"=\" * 60)\n        \n        # Load all XDF files\n        all_streams_by_file = []\n        all_file_headers = []\n        \n        for xdf_file_path in",

        "contents": "import re\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import List, Optional, Dict, Any\n\nimport cv2\nimport pandas as pd\nfrom attrs import define\n\n\n@define(slots=False)\nclass VideoMetadataParser:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parses video folders and extracts metadata including datetime from filenames.\n    \n    Usage:\n        from pypho_timeline.utils.video_metadata import VideoMetadataParser\n        \n        folder_path = Path(r\"M:\\\\ScreenRecordings\\\\EyeTrackerVR_Recordings\")\n        df = VideoMetadataParser.parse_video_folder(folder_path)\n        print(df)\n    \"\"\"\n    \n    @classmethod\n    def extract_datetime_from_filename(cls, filename: str) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Extract datetime from video filename.\n        \n        Examples:\n            'Debut_2025-07-03T230155.mp4' -&gt; datetime(2025, 7, 3, 23, 1, 55)\n            'Video_2025-12-25T120000.avi' -&gt; datetime(2025, 12, 25, 12, 0, 0)\n        \"\"\"\n        # Pattern to match: Debut_2025-07-03T230155 or similar\n        candidates = re.findall(r'\\d{4}[-_]?\\d{2}[-_]?\\d{2}[ T_-]?\\d{2}[:\\-]?\\d{2}[:\\-]?\\d{2}', filename)\n        for cand in candidates:\n            normalized = cand.replace(\"_\", \"T\").replace(\" \", \"T\")\n            for fmt in [\n                \"%Y-%m-%dT%H-%M-%S\",\n                \"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S\",\n                \"%Y-%m-%dT%H%M%S\",\n                \"%Y%m%dT%H%M%S\",\n                \"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\",\n                \"%Y%m%d-%H%M%S\",\n                \"%Y%m%d%H%M%S\"\n            ]:\n                try:\n                    return datetime.strptime(normalized, fmt)\n                except ValueError:\n                    continue\n        return None\n    \n    @classmethod\n    def get_file_metadata(cls, video_path: Path) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": "def video_metadata_to_intervals_df(video_df: pd.DataFrame, reference_timestamp: Optional[float] = None) -&gt; pd.DataFrame:\n\"\"\"\n    if video_df.empty:\n        return pd.DataFrame()\n    \n    # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp (float seconds)\n    if 'video_start_datetime' not in video_df.columns:\n        return pd.DataFrame()\n    \n    # Convert datetime to timestamp\n    timestamps = video_df['video_start_datetime'].values.astype('datetime64[ns]').astype(np.float64) / 1e9\n    \n    # Calculate t_start relative to reference or first video\n    if reference_timestamp is None:\n        reference_timestamp = float(timestamps[0])\n    \n    t_start_values = timestamps - reference_timestamp\n    \n    # Create intervals_df\n    intervals_df = pd.DataFrame({\n        't_start': t_start_values,\n        't_duration': video_df['video_duration'].values if 'video_duration' in video_df.columns else 0.0,\n        'video_file_path': video_df['video_file_path'].values if 'video_file_path' in video_df.columns else '',\n    })\n    \n    # Preserve all other metadata columns\n    metadata_cols = [col for col in video_df.columns if col not in ['video_start_datetime', 'video_end_datetime', 'video_duration', 'video_file_path']]\n    for col in metadata_cols:\n        intervals_df[col] = video_df[col].values\n    \n    return intervals_df\n\n\n# ==================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================== #",
        "signatures": {

        "contents": "if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # To demo: supply a path to a valid XDF file containing EEG/LFP/continuous apparatus streams:\n    # e.g. replace with your local file: demo_xdf_path = Path(\"/path/to/your/example.xdf\")\n    # Or, use main() for standard demo\n    import argparse\n\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=\"Run pyPhoTimeline EEG XDF example.\")\n    parser.add_argument('--xdf', type=str, help='Path to XDF file to visualize EEG tracks.')\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    if args.xdf is not None:\n        demo_xdf_path = Path(args.xdf)\n\n    else:\n        demo_xdf_path = Path(r\"E:/Dropbox (Personal)/Databases/UnparsedData/LabRecorderStudies/sub-P001/LabRecorder_Apogee_2025-10-21T051157.400Z_eeg.xdf\").resolve()\n        assert demo_xdf_path.exists()\n\n\n    if not demo_xdf_path.exists():\n        print(f\"ERROR: XDF file does not exist: {demo_xdf_path}\")\n        sys.exit(1)\n    else:\n        print(f'loading xdf file: \"{demo_xdf_path.as_posix()}\"')\n        \n    app = pg.mkQApp(\"pyPhoTimelineXDFExample\")\n    # main_all_eeg_modalities_from_xdf_file_example(demo_xdf_path)\n    main_all_modalities_from_xdf_file_example(demo_xdf_path)\n    sys.exit(app.exec_())",

        "contents": "class VideoTrackDatasource(IntervalProvidingTrackDatasource):\ndef __init__(self, video_intervals_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None, video_folder_path: Optional[Path] = None, video_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None, video_paths: Optional[List[Union[Path, str]]] = None, custom_datasource_name: Optional[str] = None, reference_timestamp: Optional[float] = None, frames_per_second: float = 10.0, thumbnail_size: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = (128, 128), use_vispy_renderer: bool = False):\n\"\"\"\n        # Determine which input method to use\n        if video_intervals_df is not None:\n            # Direct intervals DataFrame provided\n            intervals_df = video_intervals_df.copy()\n        elif video_paths is not None:\n            # Parse from list of video file paths\n            if not CV2_AVAILABLE:\n                raise ImportError(\"opencv-python is required for video parsing. Install with: pip install opencv-python\")\n            # Parse each video file individually\n            results = []\n            for video_path in video_paths:\n                video_path = Path(video_path)\n                if not video_path.exists():\n                    continue\n                # Extract datetime from filename\n                video_start_datetime = VideoMetadataParser.extract_datetime_from_filename(video_path.name)\n                if video_start_datetime is None:\n                    # If no datetime in filename, use file modification time as fallback\n                    try:\n                        video_start_datetime = datetime.fromtimestamp(video_path.stat().st_mtime)\n                    except Exception:\n                        continue\n                # Extract video metadata\n                metadata = VideoMetadataParser.extract_video_metadata(video_path)\n                if metadata is None:\n                    continue\n                # Get file metadata\n                file_metadata = VideoMetadataParser.get_file_metadata(video_path)\n                # Calculate end datetime\n                video_end_datetime = video_start_datetime + timedelta(seconds=metadata['video_duration'])\n                # Build result row\n                result =",

        "contents": "def perform_process_all_streams_multi_xdf(streams_list: List[List], xdf_file_paths: List[Path]) -&gt; Tuple[Dict, Dict]:\n\n            \n            all_intervals_dfs.append(intervals_df)\n            \n            # Create detailed DataFrame if time_series exists\n            if time_series is not None and len(time_series) &gt; 0:\n                n_channels = int(stream['info']['channel_count'][0])\n                n_t_stamps, n_columns = np.shape(time_series)\n                \n                if n_channels == n_columns and len(timestamps) == n_t_stamps:\n                    # Determine channel names based on stream type\n                    if (stream_type.upper() in ['SIGNAL', 'RAW']) and ('Motion' in stream_name):\n                        channel_names = modality_channels_dict['MOTION']\n                    elif (stream_type.upper() in ['RAW']) and (' eQuality' in stream_name):\n                        channel_names = modality_channels_dict['EEG']\n                    elif (stream_type.upper() == 'EEG'):\n                        channel_names = modality_channels_dict['EEG']\n                    elif (stream_type.upper() in ['MARKERS']) and (stream_name in ['EventBoard', 'TextLogger']):\n                        channel_names = modality_channels_dict['LOG']\n                    else:\n                        # Generic: use column indices\n                        channel_names = [f'Channel_{i}' for i in range(n_columns)]\n                    \n                    time_series_df = pd.DataFrame(time_series, columns=channel_names)\n                    time_series_df['t'] = timestamps\n                    all_detailed_dfs.append(time_series_df)\n        \n        # Check if we have valid intervals\n        if not all_intervals_dfs:\n            print(f\"  No valid intervals for stream '{stream_name}'\")\n            continue\n        \n        # Merge intervals for display (all_streams dict)\n        merged_intervals_df = pd.concat(all_intervals_dfs, ignore_index=True).sort_values('t_start')\n        all_streams[stream_name] = merged_intervals_df\n        \n        has_valid_intervals = len(merged_intervals_df) &gt;         "contents": "def perform_process_all_streams_multi_xdf(streams_list: List[List], xdf_file_paths: List[Path]) -&gt; Tuple[Dict, Dict]:\n    \"\"\"Process streams from multiple XDF files and merge streams with the same name.\n    \n    Streams with the same name across different files will be merged into a single datasource.\n    Timestamps are preserved as absolute values (no time shifting).\n    \n    Args:\n        streams_list: List of stream lists (one per XDF file), where each stream list contains\n                     stream dictionaries from pyxdf\n        xdf_file_paths: List of Path objects corresponding to each stream list\n    \n    Returns:\n        Tuple of (all_streams dict, all_streams_datasources dict) where:\n        - all_streams: Dictionary mapping stream names to merged interval DataFrames\n        - all_streams_datasources: Dictionary mapping stream names to merged TrackDatasource instances\n    \"\"\"\n    if len(streams_list) != len(xdf_file_paths):\n        raise ValueError(f\"streams_list length ({len(streams_list)}) must match xdf_file_paths length ({len(xdf_file_paths)})\")\n    \n    # Group streams by name across all files\n    streams_by_file = list(zip(streams_list, xdf_file_paths))\n    streams_by_name = merge_streams_by_name(streams_by_file)\n    \n    all_streams = {}\n    all_streams_datasources = {}\n    \n    # Process each unique stream name\n    for",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.cursor\\plans\\multi-xdf_file_support_for_timeline_tracks_537b39a1.plan.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 35
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 85
          }
        },
        "contents": "## Current Architecture Analysis\n\nThe current implementation processes a single XDF file:\n\n- &#96;TimelineBuilder.build_from_xdf_file()&#96; loads one XDF file via &#96;pyxdf.load_xdf()&#96;\n- &#96;perform_process_all_streams()&#96; processes streams and creates one datasource per stream\n- Each datasource contains data from a single stream in a single XDF file\n- Tracks are created directly from datasources\n\n## Design Decisions\n\nBased on user requirements:\n\n- **Merge Strategy**: Merge by stream name (e.g., all \"Epoc X EEG\" streams from different files → one track)\n- **Time Synchronization**: Use absolute timestamps (preserve original timestamps from each file)\n\n## Implementation Plan\n\n### 1. Extend TimelineBuilder API\n\n**File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/timeline_builder.py&#96;\n\n- Add new method &#96;build_from_xdf_files(xdf_file_paths: List[Path], ...)&#96; that accepts multiple XDF file paths\n- Keep existing &#96;build_from_xdf_file()&#96; for backward compatibility (can call new method internally)\n- The new method should:\n        - Load all XDF files\n        - Process streams from all files\n        - Merge streams by name across files\n        - Build timeline from merged datasources\n\n### 2. Extend Stream Processing\n\n**File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/widgets/simple_timeline_widget.py&#96;\n\n- Modify &#96;perform_process_all_streams()&#96; to accept an optional parameter indicating source file information\n- Create a new function &#96;perform_process_all_streams_multi_xdf(streams_list: List[List], xdf_file_paths: List[Path])&#96; that:\n        - Takes a list of stream lists (one per XDF file) and corresponding file paths\n        - Groups streams by name across all files\n        - For each unique stream name, collects all matching streams from all files\n        - Creates merged datasources that aggregate data from multiple files\n\n### 3. Enhance Datasources for Multi-File Support\n\n**Files**:\n\n- &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/specific/eeg.py&#96;\n- &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/specific/motion.py&#96;\n- &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/specific/position.py&#96;\n- &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/track_datasource.py&#96;",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\pypho_timeline\\utils\\video_metadata.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 10
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 317
          }
        },
        "contents": "@define(slots=False)\nclass VideoMetadataParser:\n@classmethod\ndef parse_video_folder(cls, folder_path: Path, video_extensions: List[str] = ['.mp4', '.avi', '.mov', '.mkv', '.wmv'], use_cache: bool = True, force_rebuild: bool = False) -&gt; pd.DataFrame:\n\n                    use_cached = True\n            \n            if not use_cached:\n                # Extract datetime from filename\n                video_start_datetime = cls.extract_datetime_from_filename(video_path.name)\n                if video_start_datetime is None:\n                    continue\n                \n                # Extract video metadata\n                metadata = cls.extract_video_metadata(video_path)\n                if metadata is None:\n                    continue\n                \n                # Get file metadata for cache validation\n                file_metadata = cls.get_file_metadata(video_path)\n                \n                # Calculate end datetime\n                video_end_datetime = video_start_datetime + timedelta(seconds=metadata['video_duration'])\n                \n                # Build result row\n                result = {\n                    'video_start_datetime': video_start_datetime,\n                    'video_duration': metadata['video_duration'],\n                    'video_end_datetime': video_end_datetime,\n                    'video_num_frames': metadata['video_num_frames'],\n                    'video_fps': metadata['video_fps'],\n                    'video_width': metadata['video_width'],\n                    'video_height': metadata['video_height'],\n                    'video_file_path': resolved_path,\n                    'video_file_size': metadata['video_file_size'],\n                    'cache_file_size': file_metadata['file_size'],\n                    'cache_file_mtime': file_metadata['file_mtime'],\n                }\n                results.append(result)\n        \n        if not results:\n            #         "contents": "- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching WhisperTrack)\n- **Color**: Cyan/teal theme (matching WhisperTrack)\n\n#### 6. XDFStreamTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;recording_datetime&#96; (or &#96;first_timestamp_dt&#96;), &#96;duration_sec&#96; (or &#96;last_timestamp_dt&#96;)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching XDFStreamTrack)\n- **Color**: Gray theme (matching XDFStreamTrack)\n\n#### 7. StringDataTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;onset&#96;, &#96;duration&#96;\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (base class, overview only)\n- **Color**: Default (subclasses set their own)\n\n### Required Methods Implementation\n\nEach datasource must implement:\n\n1. **&#96;df&#96; property**: Returns DataFrame with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;, and visualization columns\n2. **&#96;time_column_names&#96; property**: Returns &#96;['t_start', 't_duration', 't_end']&#96;\n3. **&#96;total_df_start_end_times&#96; property**: Returns &#96;(min_t_start, max_t_end)&#96; tuple\n4. **&#96;get_updated_data_window(new_start, new_end)&#96;**: Returns overlapping intervals\n5. **&#96;fetch_detailed_data(interval)&#96;**: Returns detailed data (DataFrame, dict, or None)\n6. **&#96;get_detail_renderer()&#96;**: Returns &#96;DetailRenderer&#96; instance or None\n7. **&#96;update_visualization_properties(function)&#96;**: Updates visualization columns\n\n### Helper Utilities\n\nCreate helper functions for:\n\n- Parsing duration to seconds (handle Timedelta, float, string)\n- Converting datetime to timestamp\n- Normalizing datetime columns to UTC-naive\n- Creating default pens/brushes with colors and alpha\n\n### Testing Considerations\n\nEach datasource should:\n\n- Handle empty DataFrames gracefully\n- Handle missing columns with sensible defaults\n- Convert datetime columns correctly (timezone-aware and naive)\n- Filter intervals correctly for time windows\n- Generate unique cache keys for intervals\n\n## Files to Create\n\n- &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/modality_datasources.py&#96; - All modality datasources\n\n## Files to Update\n\n- &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/__init__.py&#96; - Export new datasources",

        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.cursor\\plans\\fix_xdf_example_to_show_actual_stream_data_6b16ec81.plan.md",

        },
        "contents": "---\nname: Fix XDF example to show actual stream data\noverview: Fix the XDF example to disable example tracks and properly display XDF stream intervals like PhoOfflineEEGAnalysis does, instead of showing generic example tracks.\ntodos: []\n---\n\n# Fix XDF Example to Show Ac\n\ntual Stream Data\n\n## Problem\n\n1. &#96;SimpleTimelineWidget.setupUI()&#96; always calls &#96;add_example_tracks()&#96;, so example tracks appear even in the XDF example\n2. The XDF example creates incorrect intervals (1-second intervals for every timestamp) instead of proper stream recording intervals\n3. The intervals don't represent the actual stream data properly\n\n## Solution\n\n1. Make &#96;add_example_tracks()&#96; optional in &#96;SimpleTimelineWidget&#96;\n2. Fix XDF stream interval extraction to create proper recording intervals (one interval per stream with correct start/end times)\n3. Ensure XDF example doesn't call &#96;add_example_tracks()&#96;\n\n## Changes\n\n### File: &#96;pypho_timeline/__main__.py&#96;\n\n**1. Make &#96;add_example_tracks()&#96; optional in &#96;SimpleTimelineWidget.__init__&#96;**Add a parameter to control whether example tracks are added:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\ndef __init__(self, total_start_time=0.0, total_end_time=100.0, window_duration=10.0, window_start_time=30.0, add_example_tracks=True, parent=None):\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThen in &#96;setupUI()&#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# Add some example tracks (only if requested)\nif add_example_tracks:\n    self.add_example_tracks()\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**2. Fix XDF stream interval extraction (lines 603-622)**Replace the incorrect interval creation with proper stream intervals:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# --- 3. Build interval DataFrame for each EEG stream ---\neeg_datasources = []\nfor i, s in enumerate(eeg_streams):\n    timestamps = s['time_stamps']\n    stream_name = s['info']['name'][0]\n    n_channels = int(s['info']['channel_count'][0])\n    print(f\"Stream {i}: {stream_name}, channels: {n_channels}, samples: {len(timestamps)}\")\n\n    # Create a single interval representing the entire stream recording",

        "contents": "@define(slots=False)\nclass VideoMetadataParser:\n\n    \n    @classmethod\n    def load_cache(cls, cache_path: Path) -&gt; pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"\n        Load cached video metadata from CSV file.\n        \n        Args:\n            cache_path: Path to the cache CSV file\n            \n        Returns:\n            DataFrame with cached metadata, or empty DataFrame if cache doesn't exist or is corrupted.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not cache_path.exists():\n            return pd.DataFrame()\n        \n        try:\n            df = pd.read_csv(cache_path)\n            \n            # Parse datetime columns\n            if 'video_start_datetime' in df.columns:\n                df['video_start_datetime'] = pd.to_datetime(df['video_start_datetime'])\n            if 'video_end_datetime' in df.columns:\n                df['video_end_datetime'] = pd.to_datetime(df['video_end_datetime'])\n            \n            return df\n        except Exception:\n            # Corrupted cache - return empty DataFrame\n            return pd.DataFrame()\n    \n    @classmethod\n    def save_cache(cls, df: pd.DataFrame, cache_path: Path) -&gt; None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Save video metadata DataFrame to cache CSV file.\n        \n        Args:\n            df: DataFrame with video metadata to cache\n            cache_path: Path where cache should be saved\n        \"\"\"\n        if df.empty:\n            return\n        \n        try:\n            # Ensure parent directory exists\n            cache_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n            \n            # Save to CSV\n            df.to_csv(cache_path, index=False)\n        except Exception:\n            # Silently fail if we can't save cache\n            pass\n    \n    @classmethod\n    def extract_video_metadata(cls, video_path: Path) -&gt; Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n        ",

Make sure video dependencies are installed.\")\n        \n        # Create VideoTrackDatasource if not provided\n        if video_datasource is None:\n            video_datasource = VideoTrackDatasource(\n                video_intervals_df=video_intervals_df,\n                video_folder_path=video_folder_path,\n                video_df=video_df,\n                video_paths=video_paths,\n                custom_datasource_name=custom_datasource_name,\n                reference_timestamp=reference_timestamp,\n                frames_per_second=frames_per_second,\n                thumbnail_size=thumbnail_size\n            )\n        \n        # Check if datasource has any intervals\n        if video_datasource.intervals_df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"VideoTrackDatasource has no video intervals. Check that video files exist and are valid.\")\n        \n        # Get reference datetime (use reference_timestamp if available, otherwise fallback)\n        from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import get_earliest_reference_datetime\n        reference_datetime = None\n        if reference_timestamp is not None:\n            # Convert reference_timestamp (float) to datetime (assuming Unix epoch, UTC)\n            reference_datetime = datetime.fromtimestamp(reference_timestamp, tz=timezone.utc)\n        else:\n            # Fallback to earliest timestamp or Unix epoch\n            reference_datetime = get_earliest_reference_datetime([], [video_datasource])\n        \n        # Build timeline from the single video datasource\n        return",

        "contents": "def get_earliest_reference_datetime(file_headers: list, datasources: list) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n    \"\"\"Get the earliest reference datetime from multiple sources.\n    \n    This function tries to extract reference datetimes from XDF file headers,\n    and falls back to using the earliest timestamp from datasources if headers\n    don't contain recording start times.\n    \n    Args:\n        file_headers: List of XDF file header dictionaries\n        datasources: List of TrackDatasource instances (for fallback)\n        \n    Returns:\n        datetime object, or None if no reference can be determined\n    \"\"\"\n    reference_datetimes = []\n    \n    # Try to get from XDF headers\n    for file_header in file_headers:\n        ref_dt = get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header)\n        if ref_dt is not None:\n            reference_datetimes.append(ref_dt)\n    \n    # If we found any from headers, use the earliest one\n    if reference_datetimes:\n        return min(reference_datetimes)\n    \n    # Fallback: use earliest timestamp from datasources as Unix epoch reference\n    # This assumes timestamps are relative to Unix epoch\n    if datasources:\n        earliest_timestamp = min([ds.total_df_start_end_times[0] for ds in datasources if ds is not None])\n        # Use Unix epoch (1970-01-01 UTC) as reference (timezone-aware)\n        return UNIX_EPOCH_UTC\n    \n    # Last resort: return Unix epoch (timezone-aware, UTC)\n    logger.warning(\"No reference datetime found, using Unix epoch (1970-01-01 UTC)\")\n    return UNIX_EPOCH_UTC",

        "contents": "def float_to_datetime(timestamp: float, reference_datetime: datetime) -&gt; datetime:\n    \"\"\"Convert float timestamp to datetime using reference datetime.\n    \n    Args:\n        timestamp: Float timestamp in seconds (relative to reference)\n        reference_datetime: Reference datetime object\n        \n    Returns:\n        datetime object representing absolute time (timezone-aware, UTC)\n    \"\"\"\n    if reference_datetime is None:\n        raise ValueError(\"reference_datetime cannot be None\")\n    \n    # Make reference_datetime timezone-aware if it's naive\n    if reference_datetime.tzinfo is None:\n        # Assume UTC if naive\n        reference_datetime = reference_datetime.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n    \n    result = reference_datetime + timedelta(seconds=timestamp)\n    return result\n\n\ndef datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt: datetime) -&gt; float:\n    \"\"\"",

        },
        "contents": "---\nname: Convert time axes to datetime\noverview: Convert all x (time) axes for timeline tracks from float seconds to datetime axes, using XDF file header recording start time as the reference datetime when available.\ntodos: []\n---\n\n# Convert Time Axes to Datetime Axes\n\n## Overview\n\nConvert all x (time) axes for timeline tracks from float seconds to datetime axes. **Critical requirement**: All tracks must use a single, consistent absolute datetime reference to maintain real-time alignment, even when tracks start/stop independently. The reference datetime will be extracted from XDF file headers when available, with fallback options for other data sources.\n\n## Key Changes\n\n### 1. Add Datetime Reference Management (CRITICAL: Single Reference for Alignment)\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/timeline_builder.py&#96;\n  - Extract reference datetime from XDF file headers in &#96;build_from_xdf_files()&#96;\n  - **Use a single reference datetime for the entire timeline** - this ensures all tracks align in real time\n  - For multiple XDF files: Use the earliest recording start time as the common reference\n  - Store reference datetime in &#96;TimelineBuilder&#96; instance and pass to &#96;SimpleTimelineWidget&#96;\n  - All tracks in a timeline must use the same reference datetime for proper alignment\n  - Handle fallback when XDF header doesn't have recording start time (use Unix epoch or earliest timestamp across all datasources)\n\n### 2. Update SimpleTimelineWidget\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/widgets/simple_timeline_widget.py&#96;\n  - Add &#96;reference_datetime&#96; parameter to &#96;__init__()&#96;\n  - Store reference datetime as instance attribute\n  - Convert time values when setting ranges and labels\n\n### 3. Configure PyQtGraph DateAxisItem\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/core/pyqtgraph_time_synchronized_widget.py&#96;\n  - Replace standard bottom axis with &#96;pg.DateAxisItem&#96; in &#96;_buildGraphics()&#96;",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "---",

        "contents": "class TimelineBuilder:\ndef _add_tracks_to_timeline(self, timeline: SimpleTimelineWidget, datasources: List[TrackDatasource]) -&gt; None:\n\n            \n            # Set the plot to show the full time range (convert to datetime then Unix timestamp if reference available)\n            if timeline.reference_datetime is not None:\n                from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import float_to_datetime, datetime_to_unix_timestamp\n                dt_start = float_to_datetime(timeline.total_data_start_time, timeline.reference_datetime)\n                dt_end = float_to_datetime(timeline.total_data_end_time, timeline.reference_datetime)\n                # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp for PyQtGraph (DateAxisItem expects timestamps but displays as dates)\n                unix_start = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_start)\n                unix_end = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_end)\n                a_plot_item.setXRange(unix_start, unix_end, padding=0)\n                a_plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time')\n            else:\n                a_plot_item.setXRange(timeline.total_data_start_time, timeline.total_data_end_time, padding=0)\n                a_plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time', units='s')\n            a_plot_item.setYRange(0, 1, padding=0)\n            a_plot_item.setLabel('left', datasource.custom_datasource_name)\n            a_plot_item.hideAxis('left')  # Hide Y-axis for cleaner look\n            \n            # Add the track to the plot\n            a_track_name: str = datasource.custom_datasource_name\n            timeline.add_track(datasource, name=a_track_name, plot_item=a_plot_item)\n        ## END for datasource in datasources...\n        \n        # Hide x-axis labels for all tracks except the bottom-most one",

          }
        },
        "contents": "def video_metadata_to_intervals_df(video_df: pd.DataFrame, reference_timestamp: Optional[float] = None) -&gt; pd.DataFrame:\n\"\"\"\n    if video_df.empty:\n        return pd.DataFrame()\n    \n    # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp (float seconds)\n    if 'video_start_datetime' not in video_df.columns:\n        return pd.DataFrame()\n    \n    # Convert datetime to timestamp\n    timestamps = video_df['video_start_datetime'].values.astype('datetime64[ns]').astype(np.float64) / 1e9\n    \n    # Calculate t_start relative to reference or first video\n    if reference_timestamp is None:\n        reference_timestamp = float(timestamps[0])\n    \n    t_start_values = timestamps - reference_timestamp\n    \n    # Create intervals_df\n    intervals_df = pd.DataFrame({\n        't_start': t_start_values,\n        't_duration': video_df['video_duration'].values if 'video_duration' in video_df.columns else 0.0,\n        'video_file_path': video_df['video_file_path'].values if 'video_file_path' in video_df.columns else '',\n    })\n    \n    # Preserve all other metadata columns\n    metadata_cols = [col for col in video_df.columns if col not in ['video_start_datetime', 'video_end_datetime', 'video_duration', 'video_file_path']]\n    for col in metadata_cols:\n        intervals_df[col] = video_df[col].values\n    \n    return intervals_df\n\n\n# ==================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================== #",
        "signatures": {

        "contents": "class TimelineBuilder:\ndef build_from_video(self, video_datasource: Optional[VideoTrackDatasource] = None, video_folder_path: Optional[Path] = None, video_paths: Optional[List[Union[Path, str]]] = None, video_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None, video_intervals_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None, custom_datasource_name: Optional[str] = None, reference_timestamp: Optional[float] = None, window_duration: Optional[float] = None, window_start_time: Optional[float] = None, window_title: Optional[str] = None, window_size: Tuple[int, int] = (1000, 800), frames_per_second: float = 10.0, thumbnail_size: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = (128, 128)) -&gt; SimpleTimelineWidget:\n\"\"\"\n        if VideoTrackDatasource is None:\n            raise ImportError(\"VideoTrackDatasource is not available. Make sure video dependencies are installed.\")\n        \n        # Create VideoTrackDatasource if not provided\n        if video_datasource is None:\n            video_datasource = VideoTrackDatasource(\n                video_intervals_df=video_intervals_df,\n                video_folder_path=video_folder_path,\n                video_df=video_df,\n                video_paths=video_paths,\n                custom_datasource_name=custom_datasource_name,\n                reference_timestamp=reference_timestamp,\n                frames_per_second=frames_per_second,\n                thumbnail_size=thumbnail_size\n            )\n        \n        # Check if datasource has any intervals\n        if video_datasource.intervals_df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"VideoTrackDatasource has no video intervals. Check that video files exist and are valid.\")\n        \n        # Get reference datetime (use reference_timestamp if available, otherwise fallback)\n        from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import get_earliest_reference_datetime\n        reference_datetime = None\n        if reference_timestamp is not None:\n            # Convert reference_timestamp (float) to datetime (assuming Unix epoch, UTC)\n            reference_datetime = datetime.fromtimestamp(reference_timestamp, tz=timezone.utc)\n        else:\n            # Fallback to earliest timestamp or Unix epoch\n            reference_datetime = get_earliest_reference_datetime([], [video_datasource])\n        \n        # Build timeline from the single video datasource\n        return",

        "contents": "def main():\n\n    recent_videos = all_videos[:10]\n    recent_videos\n\n    # Create the VideoTrackDatasource\n    # video_ds = VideoTrackDatasource(video_paths=recent_videos)\n\n    print(f\"Recent videos: {recent_videos}\")\n\n    \n    \n    # Create VideoTrackDatasource with vispy renderer enabled\n    print(\"Creating VideoTrackDatasource with vispy renderer...\")\n    video_datasource = VideoTrackDatasource(\n        # video_folder_path=video_folder_path,\n        video_paths=recent_videos,\n        custom_datasource_name=\"Video Track (Vispy)\",\n        use_vispy_renderer=True,  # Enable vispy renderer\n        frames_per_second=10.0,\n        thumbnail_size=(128, 128)\n    )\n    \n    # Get time range from datasource\n    if not video_datasource.intervals_df.empty:\n        t_start, t_end = video_datasource.total_df_start_end_times\n        print(f\"Video track time range: {t_start:.2f} to {t_end:.2f} seconds\")\n        print(f\"Number of video intervals: {len(video_datasource.intervals_df)}\")\n    else:\n        # Use default time range if no videos found\n        t_start, t_end = 0.0, 100.0\n        print(\"No video intervals found, using default time range\")\n    \n    # Create reference datetime (for datetime axis alignment)\n    reference_datetime = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\n    \n    # Create the timeline widget\n    print(\"\\nCreating SimpleTimelineWidget...\")\n    timeline = SimpleTimelineWidget(\n        total_start_time=t_start,\n        total_end_time=t_end,\n        window_duration=min(30.0, (t_end - t_start) * 0.3),  # 30% of total range or 30s\n        window_start_time=t_start,\n        add_example_tracks=False,\n        reference_datetime=reference_datetime\n    )\n    \n    # Add video track with vispy renderer\n    print(\"Adding video track with vispy renderer...\")\n    video_widget, root_graphics, plot_item, dock = timeline.add_video_track(\n        track_name=\"Video Track (Vispy)\",",

        "contents": "- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/core/pyqtgraph_time_synchronized_widget.py&#96;\n  - Replace standard bottom axis with &#96;pg.DateAxisItem&#96; in &#96;_buildGraphics()&#96;\n  - Update &#96;on_window_changed()&#96; to convert float times to datetime before setting ranges\n  - Ensure all time operations use datetime-aware values\n\n### 4. Update Timeline Builder Track Creation\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/timeline_builder.py&#96;\n  - In &#96;_add_tracks_to_timeline()&#96;, configure axes with datetime formatting\n  - Convert &#96;setXRange()&#96; calls to use datetime values\n  - Update &#96;setLabel()&#96; to use datetime format instead of 's' units\n\n### 5. Update Other Axis Configuration Points\n\n- **Files to update**:\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/widgets/simple_timeline_widget.py&#96; - &#96;add_video_track()&#96; method\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/__main__.py&#96; - Main entry point axis configuration\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/docking/specific_dock_widget_mixin.py&#96; - Overview plot axes\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/docking/dynamic_dock_display_area.py&#96; - Widget axis configuration\n\n### 6. Helper Functions\n\n- **New file**: &#96;pypho_timeline/utils/datetime_helpers.py&#96;\n  - &#96;get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header)&#96; - Extract datetime from XDF header\n  - &#96;float_to_datetime(timestamp, reference_datetime)&#96; - Convert float to datetime\n  - &#96;datetime_to_float(dt, reference_datetime)&#96; - Convert datetime back to float (for internal calculations)\n\n### 7. Handle Edge Cases (Maintaining Real-Time Alignment)\n\n- **Multiple XDF files**: Use the earliest reference datetime across all files as the common reference point\n- **Mixed data sources**: When combining XDF and video tracks, use the earliest available reference datetime\n- **Video-only timelines**: Use Unix epoch or configurable reference (must be consistent across all video tracks)\n- **Missing XDF header info**: Fallback to Unix epoch (1970-01-01) or use earliest timestamp across all datasources\n- **Track start/stop independence**: Tracks can have different start/stop times, but all use the same reference datetime for alignment\n- **Maintain backward compatibility**: Internal calculations can still use float seconds (relative to reference), but display uses datetime\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### PyQtGraph DateAxisItem Usage\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python",

        "contents": "- **Maintain backward compatibility**: Internal calculations can still use float seconds (relative to reference), but display uses datetime\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### PyQtGraph DateAxisItem Usage\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nfrom pyphoplacecellanalysis.External.pyqtgraph import DateAxisItem\naxis = DateAxisItem(orientation='bottom')\nplot_item.setAxisItems({'bottom': axis})\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Time Conversion Pattern (Ensuring Alignment)\n\n- Store reference datetime as &#96;datetime.datetime&#96; object (single instance shared across all tracks)\n- Convert float timestamps to datetime: &#96;reference_datetime + timedelta(seconds=timestamp)&#96;\n- **All tracks use the same reference_datetime** - this ensures real-time alignment\n- Use datetime values for axis ranges and labels (display layer)\n- Keep internal calculations in float seconds for performance (data layer)\n- When tracks start at different times, their timestamps are converted using the same reference, so they appear at correct absolute positions on the datetime axis\n\n### XDF Header Parsing\n\n- Check &#96;file_header&#96; for recording start time\n- Common locations: &#96;file_header['info']['recording']['start_time']&#96; or similar\n- Parse ISO format or Unix timestamp format as needed",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "- **Maintain backward compatibility**: Internal calculations can still use float seconds (relative to reference), but display uses datetime",
            "lineNumber": 68

            "lineNumber": 70

          {
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "text": "### PyQtGraph DateAxisItem Usage",
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;python",
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "from pyphoplacecellanalysis.External.pyqtgraph import DateAxisItem",
            "lineNumber": 75

            "text": "axis = DateAxisItem(orientation='bottom')",

            "text": "plot_item.setAxisItems({'bottom': axis})",
            "lineNumber": 77

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\pypho_timeline\\timeline_builder.py",
        "range": {

        },
        "contents": "class TimelineBuilder:\ndef build_from_datasources(self, datasources: List[TrackDatasource], window_duration: Optional[float] = None, window_start_time: Optional[float] = None, add_example_tracks: bool = False, window_title: Optional[str] = None, window_size: Tuple[int, int] = (1000, 800), reference_datetime: Optional[datetime] = None) -&gt; SimpleTimelineWidget:\n\n        \n        # Use Unix epoch as fallback if no reference datetime provided\n        if reference_datetime is None:\n            from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import get_earliest_reference_datetime\n            reference_datetime = get_earliest_reference_datetime([], datasources)\n        \n        # Create the timeline widget with reference datetime\n        timeline = SimpleTimelineWidget(\n            total_start_time=total_start_time,\n            total_end_time=total_end_time,\n            window_duration=window_duration,\n            window_start_time=window_start_time,\n            add_example_tracks=add_example_tracks,\n            reference_datetime=reference_datetime\n        )\n        \n        # Add tracks to the timeline\n        self._add_tracks_to_timeline(timeline, datasources)\n        \n        # Configure window\n        timeline.setWindowTitle(window_title or \"pyPhoTimeline\")\n        timeline.resize(window_size[0], window_size[1])\n        timeline.show()\n        \n        # Dock log widget at bottom if it exists\n        if self.log_widget is not None:\n            # Hide standalone window before adding to dock (so it doesn't show as separate window)\n            self.log_widget.hide()\n            # Add to dock - the dock will automatically show the widget\n            _, dock = timeline.ui.dynamic_docked_widget_container.add_display_dock(identifier='log_widget', widget=self.log_widget, dockSize=(800, 200), dockAddLocationOpts=['bottom'])\n            # Explicitly show the widget to ensure it's visible in the dock\n            self.log_widget.show()\n        \n        print(\"\\nTimeline widget created with tracks:\")\n        for ds in datasources:\n            print(f\"  - {ds.custom_datasource_name}, time: {ds.total_df_start_end_times}\")\n        \n        print(\"\\nScroll on the timeline to see loaded intervals for each stream.\")\n        print(\"Close the window to exit.\\n\")\n        \n        return timeline\n    \n    def update_timeline(self, timeline: SimpleTimelineWidget, datasources: List[TrackDatasource], update_time_range: bool = True) -&gt; SimpleTimelineWidget:\n        ",

Set vispy renderer flag on datasource if requested\n            if use_vispy:\n                video_datasource.use_vispy_renderer = True\n            \n            # Get time range from datasource\n            t_start, t_end = video_datasource.total_df_start_end_times\n            if t_start == t_end:\n                # Default range if no data\n                t_start = self.total_data_start_time\n                t_end = self.total_data_end_time\n            \n            # Set plot ranges (convert to datetime then Unix timestamp if reference available)\n            if self.reference_datetime is not None:\n                dt_start = float_to_datetime(t_start, self.reference_datetime)\n                dt_end = float_to_datetime(t_end, self.reference_datetime)\n                # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp for PyQtGraph (DateAxisItem expects timestamps but displays as dates)\n                unix_start = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_start)\n                unix_end = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_end)\n                plot_item.setXRange(unix_start, unix_end, padding=0)\n                plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time')\n            else:\n                plot_item.setXRange(t_start, t_end, padding=0)\n                plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time', units='s')\n            plot_item.setYRange(0, 60, padding=0)\n            plot_item.setLabel('left', track_name)\n            plot_item.hideAxis('left')\n            \n            # Ensure TrackRenderingMixin is initialized\n            self.TrackRenderingMixin_on_buildUI()\n            \n            # Add track\n            self.add_track(video_datasource, name=track_name, plot_item=plot_item)\n            \n            # Hide x-axis labels for all tracks except the bottom-most one",

SimpleTimelineWidget:\n        \"\"\"Build a timeline widget from existing datasources.\n        \n        Args:\n            datasources: List of TrackDatasource instances\n            window_duration: Duration of the time window (default: auto-calculated from data)\n            window_start_time: Start time of the window (default: auto-calculated from data)\n            add_example_tracks: Whether to add example tracks (default: False)\n            window_title: Custom window title (default: \"pyPhoTimeline\")\n            window_size: Window size as (width, height) tuple (default: (1000, 800))\n            reference_datetime: Reference datetime for datetime axis alignment (default: None, uses Unix epoch)\n            \n        Returns:\n            SimpleTimelineWidget instance\n        \"\"\"\n        if not datasources:\n            raise ValueError(\"datasources list cannot be empty\")\n        \n        # Calculate time range from datasources\n        total_start_time: float = np.nanmin([ds.total_df_start_end_times[0] for ds in datasources])\n        total_end_time: float = np.nanmax([ds.total_df_start_end_times[1] for ds in datasources])\n        \n        # Calculate window duration if not provided\n        if window_duration is None:\n            window_duration = total_end_time - total_start_time\n            window_duration = max(window_duration, 10.0)\n        \n        # Calculate window start time if not provided\n        if window_start_time is None:\n            window_start_time = total_start_time\n        \n        # Use Unix epoch as fallback if no reference datetime provided",

Optional[SimpleTimelineWidget]:\n\n        all_file_headers = []\n        \n        for xdf_file_path in xdf_file_paths:\n            print(f\"Loading XDF file: {xdf_file_path} ...\")\n            streams, file_header = pyxdf.load_xdf(str(xdf_file_path))\n            print(f\"  Streams loaded: {[s['info']['name'][0] for s in streams]}\")\n            all_streams_by_file.append(streams)\n            all_file_headers.append(file_header)\n        \n        # Process streams from all files and merge by stream name\n        all_streams, all_streams_datasources = perform_process_all_streams_multi_xdf(streams_list=all_streams_by_file, xdf_file_paths=xdf_file_paths)\n        \n        if not all_streams:\n            print(\"No streams found.\")\n            return None\n        \n        print(f\"Found {len(all_streams)} unique stream names after merging: {[a_name for a_name in list(all_streams_datasources.keys())]}\")\n        \n        # Get active datasources\n        active_datasources_dict = {k: v for k, v in all_streams_datasources.items() if v is not None}\n        active_datasource_list = list(active_datasources_dict.values())\n        print(f\"\\tbuild active_datasources: {len(active_datasource_list)} datasources.\")\n        \n        if not active_datasource_list:\n            print(\"No valid datasources found.\")\n            return None\n        \n        # Extract reference datetime from XDF headers (for datetime axis alignment)\n        reference_datetime = get_earliest_reference_datetime(all_file_headers, active_datasource_list)\n        if reference_datetime is not None:\n            print(f\"Using reference datetime: {reference_datetime}\")\n        else:\n            print(\"Warning: No reference datetime found, using Unix epoch\")\n        \n        # Generate window title if not provided\n        if",

        "range": {

          }
        },
        "contents": "def add_video_track(self, track_name: str, video_datasource: VideoTrackDatasource, dockSize: Tuple[int, int] = (500, 80), sync_mode: SynchronizedPlotMode = SynchronizedPlotMode.TO_GLOBAL_DATA):\n\n        \n        # Set plot ranges (convert to datetime then Unix timestamp if reference available)\n        if hasattr(self, 'reference_datetime') and self.reference_datetime is not None:\n            from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import float_to_datetime, datetime_to_unix_timestamp\n            dt_start = float_to_datetime(t_start, self.reference_datetime)\n            dt_end = float_to_datetime(t_end, self.reference_datetime)\n            # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp for PyQtGraph (DateAxisItem expects timestamps but displays as dates)\n            unix_start = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_start)\n            unix_end = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_end)\n            plot_item.setXRange(unix_start, unix_end, padding=0)\n            plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time')\n        else:\n            plot_item.setXRange(t_start, t_end, padding=0)\n            plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time', units='s')\n        plot_item.setYRange(0, 60, padding=0)\n        plot_item.setLabel('left', track_name)\n        plot_item.hideAxis('left')\n        \n        # Ensure TrackRenderingMixin is initialized\n        self.TrackRenderingMixin_on_buildUI()\n        \n        # Add track\n        self.add_track(video_datasource, name=track_name, plot_item=plot_item)\n        \n        return video_widget, root_graphics, plot_item, dock\n\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # To demo: supply a path to a valid XDF file containing EEG/LFP/continuous apparatus streams:",

        "contents": "class VideoTrackDatasource(IntervalProvidingTrackDatasource):\ndef __init__(self, video_intervals_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None, video_folder_path: Optional[Path] = None, video_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None, video_paths: Optional[List[Union[Path, str]]] = None, custom_datasource_name: Optional[str] = None, reference_timestamp: Optional[float] = None, frames_per_second: float = 10.0, thumbnail_size: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = (128, 128), use_vispy_renderer: bool = False):\n\"\"\"\n        # Determine which input method to use\n        if video_intervals_df is not None:\n            # Direct intervals DataFrame provided\n            intervals_df = video_intervals_df.copy()\n        elif video_paths is not None:\n            # Parse from list of video file paths\n            if not CV2_AVAILABLE:\n                raise ImportError(\"opencv-python is required for video parsing. Install with: pip install opencv-python\")\n            # Parse each video file individually\n            results = []\n            for video_path in video_paths:\n                video_path = Path(video_path)\n                if not video_path.exists():\n                    continue\n                # Extract datetime from filename\n                video_start_datetime = VideoMetadataParser.extract_datetime_from_filename(video_path.name)\n                if video_start_datetime is None:\n                    # If no datetime in filename, use file modification time as fallback\n                    try:\n                        video_start_datetime = datetime.fromtimestamp(video_path.stat().st_mtime)\n                    except Exception:\n                        continue\n                # Extract video metadata\n                metadata = VideoMetadataParser.extract_video_metadata(video_path)\n                if metadata is None:\n                    continue\n                # Get file metadata\n                file_metadata = VideoMetadataParser.get_file_metadata(video_path)\n                # Calculate end datetime\n                video_end_datetime = video_start_datetime + timedelta(seconds=metadata['video_duration'])\n                # Build result row\n                result =",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.cursor\\plans\\create_working_track_datasources_68d6acdd.plan.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 85
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 129
          }
        },
        "contents": "### Key Conversions\n\n- **Datetime to Timestamp**: Convert pandas datetime columns to float timestamps using &#96;.timestamp()&#96; or &#96;.astype('datetime64[ns]').astype(np.float64) / 1e9&#96;\n- **Duration Handling**: Parse &#96;duration_sec&#96; (can be Timedelta, float, or string) to float seconds\n- **Interval Filtering**: Filter intervals that overlap with time windows using &#96;(t_start + t_duration &gt;= new_start) &amp; (t_start &lt;= new_end)&#96;\n\n### Specific Implementations\n\n#### 1. VideoMetadataTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;video_start_datetime&#96;, &#96;video_end_datetime&#96; (or &#96;video_start_datetime&#96; + &#96;video_duration&#96;)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: &#96;VideoThumbnailDetailRenderer&#96; (if video frames available)\n- **Color**: Blue theme (matching VideoMetadataTrack)\n\n#### 2. EEGRecordingTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;recording_datetime&#96;, &#96;duration_sec&#96; (or &#96;duration_sec_check&#96;)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching EEGRecordingTrack)\n- **Color**: Green/blue theme (matching EEGRecordingTrack)\n\n#### 3. MotionRecordingTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;recording_datetime&#96;, &#96;duration_sec&#96;\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: &#96;GenericPlotDetailRenderer&#96; or &#96;PositionPlotDetailRenderer&#96; (if position data available)\n- **Color**: Orange/red theme (matching MotionRecordingTrack)\n\n#### 4. PhoLogTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;onset&#96; (or &#96;time&#96;), &#96;duration&#96; (optional, defaults to 0 for point markers)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96; (0 duration for point markers)\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (text rendering handled by track, not datasource)\n- **Color**: Purple theme (matching PhoLogTrack)\n\n#### 5. WhisperTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;onset&#96;, &#96;duration&#96;\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching WhisperTrack)\n- **Color**: Cyan/teal theme (matching WhisperTrack)",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96; (0 duration for point markers)",

          },
          {
            "text": "- **Detail Renderer**: None (text rendering handled by track, not datasource)",

          },
          {
            "text": "- **Color**: Purple theme (matching PhoLogTrack)",
            "lineNumber": 121
          },

          },

            "text": "#### 5. WhisperTrackDatasource",
            "lineNumber": 123
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;onset&#96;, &#96;duration&#96;",

==================================================================================================================== #\n    # QT Slots                                                                                                             #\n    # ==================================================================================================================== #\n    \n    @pyqtExceptionPrintingSlot(float, float)\n    def on_window_changed(self, start_t, end_t):\n        # called when the window is updated\n        if self.enable_debug_print:\n            print(f'PyqtgraphTimeSynchronizedWidget.on_window_changed(start_t: {start_t}, end_t: {end_t})')\n        # if self.enable_debug_print:\n        #     profiler = pg.debug.Profiler(disabled=True, delayed=True)\n\n        if (start_t is not None) and (end_t is not None):\n            # Convert to datetime then to Unix timestamp if reference_datetime is available\n            # PyQtGraph's DateAxisItem expects Unix timestamps (float) but displays them as dates\n            if self.reference_datetime is not None:\n                dt_start = float_to_datetime(start_t, self.reference_datetime)\n                dt_end = float_to_datetime(end_t, self.reference_datetime)\n                # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp for PyQtGraph (safely handles timezone)\n                unix_start = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_start)\n                unix_end = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_end)\n                self.getRootPlotItem().setXRange(unix_start, unix_end, padding=0)\n            else:\n                self.getRootPlotItem().setXRange(start_t, end_t, padding=0) ## global frame\n\n        self.update(end_t, defer_render=False)\n        # if self.enable_debug_print:\n        #     profiler('Finished calling _update_plots()')\n            \n\n\n    # def mouse_clicked(self, event):\n    #     # Only handle middle mouse button\n    #     if event.button() == 2:  # Reset trace region visibility\n    #         event.accept()\n\n    # def mouse_moved(self, event):\n    #     if self.dragging and self.start_pos is not None:\n    #         # Update the trace region during dragging\n    #         self.trace_region.show()  # Show the trace region as it's being defined\n\n    # def mouse_released(self, event):\n    #     # Finalize the trace region definition\n    #     if event.button() == 2 and self.dragging:\n    #         self.dragging = False\n    #         self.start_pos = None\n    #         print(f\"Trace region set to: {self.trace_region.getRegion()}\")\n            \n\n\n    def getRootLayout(self) -&gt; QtWidgets.QGridLayout:\n        return self.ui.layout\n    \n    def getRootGraphicsLayoutWidget(self) -&gt; pg.GraphicsLayoutWidget:\n        return self.ui.root_graphics_layout_widget",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\pypho_timeline\\rendering\\datasources\\track_datasource.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 321
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 478
          }
        },
        "contents": "class IntervalProvidingTrackDatasource(BaseTrackDatasource):\n\n    \n\n    def fetch_detailed_data(self, interval: pd.Series) -&gt; pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"Fetch position data for an interval with optional downsampling.\"\"\"\n        if self.detailed_df is None:\n            return pd.DataFrame()  # Return empty DataFrame if no position data available\n        t_start = interval['t_start']\n        t_end = t_start + interval['t_duration']\n        mask = (self.detailed_df['t'] &gt;= t_start) &amp; (self.detailed_df['t'] &lt; t_end)\n        result_df = self.detailed_df[mask].copy()\n        \n        # Apply downsampling if enabled\n        if self.enable_downsampling and (self.max_points_per_second is not None) and (len(result_df) &gt; 0):\n            # Calculate target point count based on interval duration\n            t_duration: float = interval.get('t_duration', (t_end - t_start))\n            max_allowed_points: int = int(t_duration * self.max_points_per_second) ## compute the max allowed points in the current interval\n            curr_df_points_per_sec: float = float(len(result_df['t']))/t_duration\n\n            # Only downsample if we have more points than target\n            if len(result_df) &gt; max_allowed_points:\n                from pypho_timeline.utils.downsampling import downsample_dataframe\n                result_df = downsample_dataframe(result_df, max_points=max_allowed_points, time_col='t')\n                curr_downsampled_points_per_sec = float(len(result_df['t']))/t_duration\n\n        return result_df\n    \n\n    def get_detail_renderer(self):\n        \"\"\"Get detail renderer for position data.\"\"\"",
        "signatures": {

        "contents": "def get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header: dict) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n\n                            continue\n                elif isinstance(value, str):\n                    # Try ISO format or other string formats\n                    try:\n                        # Try ISO format first\n                        dt = datetime.fromisoformat(value.replace('Z', '+00:00'))\n                        # Make timezone-aware if naive\n                        if dt.tzinfo is None:\n                            dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n                        return dt\n                    except ValueError:\n                        # Try other common formats\n                        for fmt in ['%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S', '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%f']:\n                            try:\n                                dt = datetime.strptime(value, fmt)\n                                # Make timezone-aware (assume UTC)\n                                dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n                                return dt\n                            except ValueError:\n                                continue\n                        logger.warning(f\"Could not parse datetime string: {value}\")\n                        continue\n                elif isinstance(value, datetime):\n                    # Make timezone-aware if naive\n                    if value.tzinfo is None:\n                        value = value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n                    return value\n        except (KeyError, TypeError, AttributeError) as e:\n            continue\n    \n    logger.debug(\"Could not find recording start time in XDF header\")\n    return None\n\n\ndef float_to_datetime(timestamp: float, reference_datetime: datetime) -&gt; datetime:\n    ",
