        "contents": "def get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header: dict) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n\n    \n    for path in possible_paths:\n        try:\n            value = file_header\n            for key in path:\n                if isinstance(value, dict) and key in value:\n                    value = value[key]\n                    # XDF headers often have values as lists with single element\n                    if isinstance(value, list) and len(value) &gt; 0:\n                        value = value[0]\n                else:\n                    value = None\n                    break\n            \n            if value is not None:\n                # Try to parse as datetime\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)):\n                    # Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch)\n                    try:\n                        dt = datetime.fromtimestamp(value, tz=timezone.utc)\n                        return dt\n                    except (ValueError, OSError):\n                        # Try as milliseconds if seconds fails\n                        try:\n                            dt = datetime.fromtimestamp(value / 1000.0, tz=timezone.utc)\n                            return dt\n                        except (ValueError, OSError):\n                            logger.warning(f\"Could not parse timestamp value {value} as Unix timestamp\")\n                            continue\n                elif isinstance(value, str):\n                    # Try ISO format or other string formats\n                    try",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\pypho_timeline\\docking\\specific_dock_widget_mixin.py",

        "contents": "class SpecificDockWidgetManipulatingMixin:\n@function_attributes(short_name=None, tags=['interval', 'tracks', 'pyqtgraph', 'specific', 'dynamic_ui', 'group_matplotlib_render_plot_widget'], input_requires=[], output_provides=[], uses=['self.add_new_embedded_pyqtgraph_render_plot_widget'], used_by=[], creation_date='2025-01-09 10:50', related_items=[])\ndef prepare_pyqtgraph_intervalPlot_tracks(self, enable_interval_overview_track: bool = False, should_remove_all_and_re_add: bool=True, name_modifier_suffix: str='', should_link_to_main_plot_widget:bool=True, interval_dock_max_height: int=89, sync_mode:SynchronizedPlotMode=None, debug_print=False):\n\n            if hasattr(self, 'total_data_start_time') and hasattr(self, 'total_data_end_time'):\n                # Convert to datetime then Unix timestamp if reference_datetime is available\n                if hasattr(self, 'reference_datetime') and self.reference_datetime is not None:\n                    from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import float_to_datetime, datetime_to_unix_timestamp\n                    dt_start = float_to_datetime(self.total_data_start_time, self.reference_datetime)\n                    dt_end = float_to_datetime(self.total_data_end_time, self.reference_datetime)\n                    # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp for PyQtGraph (DateAxisItem expects timestamps but displays as dates)\n                    unix_start = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_start)\n                    unix_end = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_end)\n                    intervals_overview_plot_item.setXRange(unix_start, unix_end, padding=0)\n                else:\n                    intervals_overview_plot_item.setXRange(self.total_data_start_time, self.total_data_end_time, padding=0) ## global frame\n            \n\n        ## Enables creating a new pyqtgraph-based track to display the intervals/epochs\n        interval_window_dock_config = CustomCyclicColorsDockDisplayConfig(named_color_scheme=NamedColorScheme.grey, showCloseButton=False, corner_radius='0px', hideTitleBar=True)\n        identifier_name = f'intervals{name_modifier_suffix}'\n        intervals_time_sync_pyqtgraph_widget, intervals_root_graphics_layout_widget, intervals_plot_item, intervals_dock = self.add_new_embedded_pyqtgraph_render_plot_widget(name=identifier_name, dockSize=(10, 4), display_config=interval_window_dock_config)\n        if (interval_dock_max_height is not None) and (interval_dock_max_height &gt; 0):\n            intervals_dock.setMaximumHeight(interval_dock_max_height)\n            \n        if",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.cursor\\plans\\create_working_track_datasources_68d6acdd.plan.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 90
          }
        },
        "contents": "    status: completed\n    dependencies:\n      - implement_helper_utilities\n  - id: update_init_exports\n    content: Update __init__.py to export all new datasources\n    status: completed\n    dependencies:\n      - create_modality_datasources_file\n---\n\n# Create Working Track Datasources for pyPhoTimeline\n\n## Overview\n\nCreate working datasource implementations for &#96;pyPhoTimeline&#96; that derive from &#96;BaseTrackDatasource&#96;. These will replace the non-working implementations in &#96;PhoOfflineEEGAnalysis&#96; timeline tracks. Each datasource will convert from the timeline track data formats (datetime-based) to the &#96;BaseTrackDatasource&#96; interface (float timestamp-based).\n\n## Modalities to Implement\n\nBased on the timeline tracks, create datasources for:\n\n1. **VideoMetadataTrackDatasource** - Video recordings\n2. **EEGRecordingTrackDatasource** - EEG recordings  \n3. **MotionRecordingTrackDatasource** - Motion recordings\n4. **PhoLogTrackDatasource** - PHO_LOG annotations\n5. **WhisperTrackDatasource** - Whisper transcripts\n6. **XDFStreamTrackDatasource** - Generic XDF streams\n7. **StringDataTrackDatasource** - Generic string/comment data (base class)\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### File Structure\n\nCreate a new file: &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/modality_datasources.py&#96;This file will contain all modality-specific datasources.\n\n### Common Pattern\n\nEach datasource will:\n\n1. Accept data in the format expected by the corresponding timeline track (DataFrame with datetime columns)\n2. Convert datetime columns to float timestamps (&#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;)\n3. Create visualization columns (&#96;series_vertical_offset&#96;, &#96;series_height&#96;, &#96;pen&#96;, &#96;brush&#96;)\n4. Implement all required &#96;BaseTrackDatasource&#96; abstract methods\n5. Provide appropriate detail renderers (or None for tracks without detail views)\n\n### Key Conversions\n\n- **Datetime to Timestamp**: Convert pandas datetime columns to float timestamps using &#96;.timestamp()&#96; or &#96;.astype('datetime64[ns]').astype(np.float64) / 1e9&#96;\n- **Duration Handling**: Parse &#96;duration_sec&#96; (can be Timedelta, float, or string) to float seconds",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "    status: completed",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    dependencies:",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },
          {
            "text": "      - implement_helper_utilities",
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "text": "  - id: update_init_exports",
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "text": "    content: Update __init__.py to export all new datasources",
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "text": "    status: completed",
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "text": "    dependencies:",
            "lineNumber": 49

            "text": "      - create_modality_datasources_file",
            "lineNumber": 50

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching WhisperTrack)\n- **Color**: Cyan/teal theme (matching WhisperTrack)\n\n#### 6. XDFStreamTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;recording_datetime&#96; (or &#96;first_timestamp_dt&#96;), &#96;duration_sec&#96; (or &#96;last_timestamp_dt&#96;)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching XDFStreamTrack)\n- **Color**: Gray theme (matching XDFStreamTrack)\n\n#### 7. StringDataTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;onset&#96;, &#96;duration&#96;\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (base class, overview only)\n- **Color**: Default (subclasses set their own)\n\n### Required Methods Implementation\n\nEach datasource must implement:\n\n1. **&#96;df&#96; property**: Returns DataFrame with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;, and visualization columns\n2. **&#96;time_column_names&#96; property**: Returns &#96;['t_start', 't_duration', 't_end']&#96;\n3. **&#96;total_df_start_end_times&#96; property**: Returns &#96;(min_t_start, max_t_end)&#96; tuple\n4. **&#96;get_updated_data_window(new_start, new_end)&#96;**: Returns overlapping intervals\n5. **&#96;fetch_detailed_data(interval)&#96;**: Returns detailed data (DataFrame, dict, or None)\n6. **&#96;get_detail_renderer()&#96;**: Returns &#96;DetailRenderer&#96; instance or None\n7. **&#96;update_visualization_properties(function)&#96;**: Updates visualization columns\n\n### Helper Utilities\n\nCreate helper functions for:\n\n- Parsing duration to seconds (handle Timedelta, float, string)\n- Converting datetime to timestamp\n- Normalizing datetime columns to UTC-naive\n- Creating default pens/brushes with colors and alpha\n\n### Testing Considerations\n\nEach datasource should:\n\n- Handle empty DataFrames gracefully\n- Handle missing columns with sensible defaults\n- Convert datetime columns correctly (timezone-aware and naive)\n- Filter intervals correctly for time windows\n- Generate unique cache keys for intervals\n\n## Files to Create\n\n- &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/modality_datasources.py&#96; - All modality datasources\n\n## Files to Update\n\n- &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/__init__.py&#96; - Export new datasources",

        },
        "contents": "---\nname: Convert time axes to datetime\noverview: Convert all x (time) axes for timeline tracks from float seconds to datetime axes, using XDF file header recording start time as the reference datetime when available.\ntodos: []\n---\n\n# Convert Time Axes to Datetime Axes\n\n## Overview\n\nConvert all x (time) axes for timeline tracks from float seconds to datetime axes. **Critical requirement**: All tracks must use a single, consistent absolute datetime reference to maintain real-time alignment, even when tracks start/stop independently. The reference datetime will be extracted from XDF file headers when available, with fallback options for other data sources.\n\n## Key Changes\n\n### 1. Add Datetime Reference Management (CRITICAL: Single Reference for Alignment)\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/timeline_builder.py&#96;\n  - Extract reference datetime from XDF file headers in &#96;build_from_xdf_files()&#96;\n  - **Use a single reference datetime for the entire timeline** - this ensures all tracks align in real time\n  - For multiple XDF files: Use the earliest recording start time as the common reference\n  - Store reference datetime in &#96;TimelineBuilder&#96; instance and pass to &#96;SimpleTimelineWidget&#96;\n  - All tracks in a timeline must use the same reference datetime for proper alignment\n  - Handle fallback when XDF header doesn't have recording start time (use Unix epoch or earliest timestamp across all datasources)\n\n### 2. Update SimpleTimelineWidget\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/widgets/simple_timeline_widget.py&#96;\n  - Add &#96;reference_datetime&#96; parameter to &#96;__init__()&#96;\n  - Store reference datetime as instance attribute\n  - Convert time values when setting ranges and labels\n\n### 3. Configure PyQtGraph DateAxisItem\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/core/pyqtgraph_time_synchronized_widget.py&#96;\n  - Replace standard bottom axis with &#96;pg.DateAxisItem&#96; in &#96;_buildGraphics()&#96;",

        "contents": "- **Maintain backward compatibility**: Internal calculations can still use float seconds (relative to reference), but display uses datetime\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### PyQtGraph DateAxisItem Usage\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nfrom pyphoplacecellanalysis.External.pyqtgraph import DateAxisItem\naxis = DateAxisItem(orientation='bottom')\nplot_item.setAxisItems({'bottom': axis})\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Time Conversion Pattern (Ensuring Alignment)\n\n- Store reference datetime as &#96;datetime.datetime&#96; object (single instance shared across all tracks)\n- Convert float timestamps to datetime: &#96;reference_datetime + timedelta(seconds=timestamp)&#96;\n- **All tracks use the same reference_datetime** - this ensures real-time alignment\n- Use datetime values for axis ranges and labels (display layer)\n- Keep internal calculations in float seconds for performance (data layer)\n- When tracks start at different times, their timestamps are converted using the same reference, so they appear at correct absolute positions on the datetime axis\n\n### XDF Header Parsing\n\n- Check &#96;file_header&#96; for recording start time\n- Common locations: &#96;file_header['info']['recording']['start_time']&#96; or similar\n- Parse ISO format or Unix timestamp format as needed",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": "def datetime_to_float(dt: datetime, reference_datetime: datetime) -&gt; float:\n    \"\"\"Convert datetime back to float timestamp relative to reference.\n    \n    Args:\n        dt: datetime object representing absolute time\n        reference_datetime: Reference datetime object\n        \n    Returns:\n        Float timestamp in seconds (relative to reference)\n    \"\"\"\n    if reference_datetime is None:\n        raise ValueError(\"reference_datetime cannot be None\")\n    delta = dt - reference_datetime\n    return delta.total_seconds()\n\n\ndef get_earliest_reference_datetime(file_headers: list, datasources: list) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n    ",

        "contents": "- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/core/pyqtgraph_time_synchronized_widget.py&#96;\n  - Replace standard bottom axis with &#96;pg.DateAxisItem&#96; in &#96;_buildGraphics()&#96;\n  - Update &#96;on_window_changed()&#96; to convert float times to datetime before setting ranges\n  - Ensure all time operations use datetime-aware values\n\n### 4. Update Timeline Builder Track Creation\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/timeline_builder.py&#96;\n  - In &#96;_add_tracks_to_timeline()&#96;, configure axes with datetime formatting\n  - Convert &#96;setXRange()&#96; calls to use datetime values\n  - Update &#96;setLabel()&#96; to use datetime format instead of 's' units\n\n### 5. Update Other Axis Configuration Points\n\n- **Files to update**:\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/widgets/simple_timeline_widget.py&#96; - &#96;add_video_track()&#96; method\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/__main__.py&#96; - Main entry point axis configuration\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/docking/specific_dock_widget_mixin.py&#96; - Overview plot axes\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/docking/dynamic_dock_display_area.py&#96; - Widget axis configuration\n\n### 6. Helper Functions\n\n- **New file**: &#96;pypho_timeline/utils/datetime_helpers.py&#96;\n  - &#96;get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header)&#96; - Extract datetime from XDF header\n  - &#96;float_to_datetime(timestamp, reference_datetime)&#96; - Convert float to datetime\n  - &#96;datetime_to_float(dt, reference_datetime)&#96; - Convert datetime back to float (for internal calculations)\n\n### 7. Handle Edge Cases (Maintaining Real-Time Alignment)\n\n- **Multiple XDF files**: Use the earliest reference datetime across all files as the common reference point\n- **Mixed data sources**: When combining XDF and video tracks, use the earliest available reference datetime\n- **Video-only timelines**: Use Unix epoch or configurable reference (must be consistent across all video tracks)\n- **Missing XDF header info**: Fallback to Unix epoch (1970-01-01) or use earliest timestamp across all datasources\n- **Track start/stop independence**: Tracks can have different start/stop times, but all use the same reference datetime for alignment\n- **Maintain backward compatibility**: Internal calculations can still use float seconds (relative to reference), but display uses datetime\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### PyQtGraph DateAxisItem Usage\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\pypho_timeline\\timeline_builder.py",

        "contents": "class TimelineBuilder:\ndef _add_tracks_to_timeline(self, timeline: SimpleTimelineWidget, datasources: List[TrackDatasource]) -&gt; None:\n\n            \n            # Set the plot to show the full time range (convert to datetime then Unix timestamp if reference available)\n            if timeline.reference_datetime is not None:\n                from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import float_to_datetime, datetime_to_unix_timestamp\n                dt_start = float_to_datetime(timeline.total_data_start_time, timeline.reference_datetime)\n                dt_end = float_to_datetime(timeline.total_data_end_time, timeline.reference_datetime)\n                # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp for PyQtGraph (DateAxisItem expects timestamps but displays as dates)\n                unix_start = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_start)\n                unix_end = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_end)\n                a_plot_item.setXRange(unix_start, unix_end, padding=0)\n                a_plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time')\n            else:\n                a_plot_item.setXRange(timeline.total_data_start_time, timeline.total_data_end_time, padding=0)\n                a_plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time', units='s')\n            a_plot_item.setYRange(0, 1, padding=0)\n            a_plot_item.setLabel('left', datasource.custom_datasource_name)\n            a_plot_item.hideAxis('left')  # Hide Y-axis for cleaner look\n            \n            # Add the track to the plot\n            a_track_name: str = datasource.custom_datasource_name\n            timeline.add_track(datasource, name=a_track_name, plot_item=a_plot_item)\n        ## END for datasource in datasources...\n        \n        # Hide x-axis labels for all tracks except the bottom-most one",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\pypho_timeline\\utils\\datetime_helpers.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 199,
            "column": 32
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 237,
            "column": 25
          }
        },
        "contents": "def get_earliest_reference_datetime(file_headers: list, datasources: list) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n    \"\"\"Get the earliest reference datetime from multiple sources.\n    \n    This function tries to extract reference datetimes from XDF file headers,\n    and falls back to using the earliest timestamp from datasources if headers\n    don't contain recording start times.\n    \n    Args:\n        file_headers: List of XDF file header dictionaries\n        datasources: List of TrackDatasource instances (for fallback)\n        \n    Returns:\n        datetime object, or None if no reference can be determined\n    \"\"\"\n    reference_datetimes = []\n    \n    # Try to get from XDF headers\n    for file_header in file_headers:\n        ref_dt = get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header)\n        if ref_dt is not None:\n            reference_datetimes.append(ref_dt)\n    \n    # If we found any from headers, use the earliest one\n    if reference_datetimes:\n        return min(reference_datetimes)\n    \n    # Fallback: use earliest timestamp from datasources as Unix epoch reference\n    # This assumes timestamps are relative to Unix epoch\n    if datasources:\n        earliest_timestamp = min([ds.total_df_start_end_times[0] for ds in datasources if ds is not None])\n        # Use Unix epoch (1970-01-01 UTC) as reference (timezone-aware)\n        return UNIX_EPOCH_UTC\n    \n    # Last resort: return Unix epoch (timezone-aware, UTC)\n    logger.warning(\"No reference datetime found, using Unix epoch (1970-01-01 UTC)\")\n    return UNIX_EPOCH_UTC",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [

        "contents": "def get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header: dict) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n\n    \n    for path in possible_paths:\n        try:\n            value = file_header\n            for key in path:\n                if isinstance(value, dict) and key in value:\n                    value = value[key]\n                    # XDF headers often have values as lists with single element\n                    if isinstance(value, list) and len(value) &gt; 0:\n                        value = value[0]\n                else:\n                    value = None\n                    break\n            \n            if value is not None:\n                # Try to parse as datetime\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)):\n                    # Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch)\n                    try:\n                        dt = datetime.fromtimestamp(value, tz=timezone.utc)\n                        return dt\n                    except (ValueError, OSError):\n                        # Try as milliseconds if seconds fails\n                        try:\n                            dt = datetime.fromtimestamp(value / 1000.0, tz=timezone.utc)\n                            return dt\n                        except (ValueError, OSError):\n                            logger.warning(f\"Could not parse timestamp value {value} as Unix timestamp\")\n                            continue\n                elif isinstance(value, str):\n                    # Try ISO format or other string formats\n                    try",

        "contents": "def datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt: datetime) -&gt; float:\n    \"\"\"Convert datetime to Unix timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01 UTC).\n    \n    This function safely handles both naive and timezone-aware datetimes.\n    \n    Args:\n        dt: datetime object (naive or timezone-aware)\n        \n    Returns:\n        Unix timestamp as float (seconds since epoch)\n    \"\"\"\n    # If naive, assume UTC\n    if dt.tzinfo is None:\n        dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n    \n    return dt.timestamp()\n\n\ndef datetime_to_float(dt: datetime, reference_datetime: datetime) -&gt; float:\n    ",

        "contents": "class TimelineBuilder:\ndef build_from_datasources(self, datasources: List[TrackDatasource], window_duration: Optional[float] = None, window_start_time: Optional[float] = None, add_example_tracks: bool = False, window_title: Optional[str] = None, window_size: Tuple[int, int] = (1000, 800), reference_datetime: Optional[datetime] = None) -&gt; SimpleTimelineWidget:\n\n        \n        # Use Unix epoch as fallback if no reference datetime provided\n        if reference_datetime is None:\n            from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import get_earliest_reference_datetime\n            reference_datetime = get_earliest_reference_datetime([], datasources)\n        \n        # Create the timeline widget with reference datetime\n        timeline = SimpleTimelineWidget(\n            total_start_time=total_start_time,\n            total_end_time=total_end_time,\n            window_duration=window_duration,\n            window_start_time=window_start_time,\n            add_example_tracks=add_example_tracks,\n            reference_datetime=reference_datetime\n        )\n        \n        # Add tracks to the timeline\n        self._add_tracks_to_timeline(timeline, datasources)\n        \n        # Configure window\n        timeline.setWindowTitle(window_title or \"pyPhoTimeline\")\n        timeline.resize(window_size[0], window_size[1])\n        timeline.show()\n        \n        # Dock log widget at bottom if it exists\n        if self.log_widget is not None:\n            # Hide standalone window before adding to dock (so it doesn't show as separate window)\n            self.log_widget.hide()\n            # Add to dock - the dock will automatically show the widget\n            _, dock = timeline.ui.dynamic_docked_widget_container.add_display_dock(identifier='log_widget', widget=self.log_widget, dockSize=(800, 200), dockAddLocationOpts=['bottom'])\n            # Explicitly show the widget to ensure it's visible in the dock\n            self.log_widget.show()\n        \n        print(\"\\nTimeline widget created with tracks:\")\n        for ds in datasources:\n            print(f\"  - {ds.custom_datasource_name}, time: {ds.total_df_start_end_times}\")\n        \n        print(\"\\nScroll on the timeline to see loaded intervals for each stream.\")\n        print(\"Close the window to exit.\\n\")\n        \n        return timeline\n    \n    def update_timeline(self, timeline: SimpleTimelineWidget, datasources: List[TrackDatasource], update_time_range: bool = True) -&gt; SimpleTimelineWidget:\n        ",

        "contents": "def video_metadata_to_intervals_df(video_df: pd.DataFrame, reference_timestamp: Optional[float] = None) -&gt; pd.DataFrame:\n\"\"\"\n    if video_df.empty:\n        return pd.DataFrame()\n    \n    # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp (float seconds)\n    if 'video_start_datetime' not in video_df.columns:\n        return pd.DataFrame()\n    \n    # Convert datetime to timestamp\n    timestamps = video_df['video_start_datetime'].values.astype('datetime64[ns]').astype(np.float64) / 1e9\n    \n    # Calculate t_start relative to reference or first video\n    if reference_timestamp is None:\n        reference_timestamp = float(timestamps[0])\n    \n    t_start_values = timestamps - reference_timestamp\n    \n    # Create intervals_df\n    intervals_df = pd.DataFrame({\n        't_start': t_start_values,\n        't_duration': video_df['video_duration'].values if 'video_duration' in video_df.columns else 0.0,\n        'video_file_path': video_df['video_file_path'].values if 'video_file_path' in video_df.columns else '',\n    })\n    \n    # Preserve all other metadata columns\n    metadata_cols = [col for col in video_df.columns if col not in ['video_start_datetime', 'video_end_datetime', 'video_duration', 'video_file_path']]\n    for col in metadata_cols:\n        intervals_df[col] = video_df[col].values\n    \n    return intervals_df\n\n\n# ==================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================== #",

            "text": "        'video_file_path': video_df['video_file_path'].values if 'video_file_path' in video_df.columns else '',",
            "lineNumber": 54
          },

            "text": "    })",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },

            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Preserve all other metadata columns",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },

            "text": "    metadata_cols = [col for col in video_df.columns if col not in ['video_start_datetime', 'video_end_datetime', 'video_duration', 'video_file_path']]",

        "contents": "def create_am_pm_date_axis(orientation='bottom'):\n\"\"\"\n    try:\n        import pyphoplacecellanalysis.External.pyqtgraph as pg\n        from pyphoplacecellanalysis.External.pyqtgraph import DateAxisItem\n        \n        class AMPMDateAxisItem(DateAxisItem):\n            \"\"\"Custom DateAxisItem that displays time in 12-hour AM/PM format.\"\"\"\n            \n            def tickStrings(self, values, scale, spacing):\n                \"\"\"Override to format tick labels in 12-hour AM/PM format.\"\"\"\n                # Convert timestamps to datetime objects\n                dt_values = [datetime.fromtimestamp(value, tz=timezone.utc) for value in values]\n                # Format datetime objects to 12-hour format with AM/PM\n                # Use format like \"01/09 02:33 PM\" for date and time\n                return [dt.strftime('%m/%d %I:%M %p') for dt in dt_values]\n        \n        return AMPMDateAxisItem(orientation=orientation)\n    except (ImportError, AttributeError):\n        # Fallback: try to get DateAxisItem directly from pg module\n        try:\n            import pyphoplacecellanalysis.External.pyqtgraph as pg\n            if hasattr(pg, 'DateAxisItem'):\n                class AMPMDateAxisItem(pg.DateAxisItem):\n                    def tickStrings(self, values, scale, spacing):\n                        dt_values = [datetime.fromtimestamp(value, tz=timezone.utc) for value in values]\n                        return [dt.strftime('%m/%d %I:%M %p') for dt in dt_values]\n                return AMPMDateAxisItem(orientation=orientation)\n        except:\n            pass\n        # If all else fails, return None\n        return None\n\n\ndef get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header: dict) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n    ",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "class SimpleTimelineWidget(TrackRenderingMixin, SpecificDockWidgetManipulatingMixin, QtWidgets.QWidget):\ndef add_video_track(self, track_name: str, video_datasource: VideoTrackDatasource, dockSize: Tuple[int, int] = (500, 80), sync_mode: SynchronizedPlotMode = SynchronizedPlotMode.TO_GLOBAL_DATA, use_vispy: bool = False):\n\n            )\n            \n            # Set vispy renderer flag on datasource if requested\n            if use_vispy:\n                video_datasource.use_vispy_renderer = True\n            \n            # Get time range from datasource\n            t_start, t_end = video_datasource.total_df_start_end_times\n            if t_start == t_end:\n                # Default range if no data\n                t_start = self.total_data_start_time\n                t_end = self.total_data_end_time\n            \n            # Set plot ranges (convert to datetime then Unix timestamp if reference available)\n            if self.reference_datetime is not None:\n                dt_start = float_to_datetime(t_start, self.reference_datetime)\n                dt_end = float_to_datetime(t_end, self.reference_datetime)\n                # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp for PyQtGraph (DateAxisItem expects timestamps but displays as dates)\n                unix_start = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_start)\n                unix_end = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_end)\n                plot_item.setXRange(unix_start, unix_end, padding=0)\n                plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time')\n            else:\n                plot_item.setXRange(t_start, t_end, padding=0)\n                plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time', units='s')\n            plot_item.setYRange(0, 60, padding=0)\n            plot_item.setLabel('left', track_name)\n            plot_item.hideAxis('left')\n            \n            # Ensure TrackRenderingMixin is initialized\n            self.TrackRenderingMixin_on_buildUI()\n            \n            # Add track\n            self.add_track(video_datasource, name=track_name, plot_item=plot_item)\n            \n            # Hide x-axis labels for all tracks except the bottom-most one",

        "contents": "def get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header: dict) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n\n                            continue\n                elif isinstance(value, str):\n                    # Try ISO format or other string formats\n                    try:\n                        # Try ISO format first\n                        dt = datetime.fromisoformat(value.replace('Z', '+00:00'))\n                        # Make timezone-aware if naive\n                        if dt.tzinfo is None:\n                            dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n                        return dt\n                    except ValueError:\n                        # Try other common formats\n                        for fmt in ['%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S', '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%f']:\n                            try:\n                                dt = datetime.strptime(value, fmt)\n                                # Make timezone-aware (assume UTC)\n                                dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n                                return dt\n                            except ValueError:\n                                continue\n                        logger.warning(f\"Could not parse datetime string: {value}\")\n                        continue\n                elif isinstance(value, datetime):\n                    # Make timezone-aware if naive\n                    if value.tzinfo is None:\n                        value = value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n                    return value\n        except (KeyError, TypeError, AttributeError) as e:\n            continue\n    \n    logger.debug(\"Could not find recording start time in XDF header\")\n    return None\n\n\ndef float_to_datetime(timestamp: float, reference_datetime: datetime) -&gt; datetime:\n    ",

        "contents": "class SpecificDockWidgetManipulatingMixin:\n@function_attributes(short_name=None, tags=['interval', 'tracks', 'pyqtgraph', 'specific', 'dynamic_ui', 'group_matplotlib_render_plot_widget'], input_requires=[], output_provides=[], uses=['self.add_new_embedded_pyqtgraph_render_plot_widget'], used_by=[], creation_date='2025-01-09 10:50', related_items=[])\ndef prepare_pyqtgraph_intervalPlot_tracks(self, enable_interval_overview_track: bool = False, should_remove_all_and_re_add: bool=True, name_modifier_suffix: str='', should_link_to_main_plot_widget:bool=True, interval_dock_max_height: int=89, sync_mode:SynchronizedPlotMode=None, debug_print=False):\n\n            if hasattr(self, 'total_data_start_time') and hasattr(self, 'total_data_end_time'):\n                # Convert to datetime then Unix timestamp if reference_datetime is available\n                if hasattr(self, 'reference_datetime') and self.reference_datetime is not None:\n                    from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import float_to_datetime, datetime_to_unix_timestamp\n                    dt_start = float_to_datetime(self.total_data_start_time, self.reference_datetime)\n                    dt_end = float_to_datetime(self.total_data_end_time, self.reference_datetime)\n                    # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp for PyQtGraph (DateAxisItem expects timestamps but displays as dates)\n                    unix_start = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_start)\n                    unix_end = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_end)\n                    intervals_overview_plot_item.setXRange(unix_start, unix_end, padding=0)\n                else:\n                    intervals_overview_plot_item.setXRange(self.total_data_start_time, self.total_data_end_time, padding=0) ## global frame\n            \n\n        ## Enables creating a new pyqtgraph-based track to display the intervals/epochs\n        interval_window_dock_config = CustomCyclicColorsDockDisplayConfig(named_color_scheme=NamedColorScheme.grey, showCloseButton=False, corner_radius='0px', hideTitleBar=True)\n        identifier_name = f'intervals{name_modifier_suffix}'\n        intervals_time_sync_pyqtgraph_widget, intervals_root_graphics_layout_widget, intervals_plot_item, intervals_dock = self.add_new_embedded_pyqtgraph_render_plot_widget(name=identifier_name, dockSize=(10, 4), display_config=interval_window_dock_config)\n        if (interval_dock_max_height is not None) and (interval_dock_max_height &gt; 0):\n            intervals_dock.setMaximumHeight(interval_dock_max_height)\n            \n        if",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\pypho_timeline\\__main__.py",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {

            "line": 167,
            "column": 97
          }
        },
        "contents": "def add_video_track(self, track_name: str, video_datasource: VideoTrackDatasource, dockSize: Tuple[int, int] = (500, 80), sync_mode: SynchronizedPlotMode = SynchronizedPlotMode.TO_GLOBAL_DATA):\n\n        \n        # Set plot ranges (convert to datetime then Unix timestamp if reference available)\n        if hasattr(self, 'reference_datetime') and self.reference_datetime is not None:\n            from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import float_to_datetime, datetime_to_unix_timestamp\n            dt_start = float_to_datetime(t_start, self.reference_datetime)\n            dt_end = float_to_datetime(t_end, self.reference_datetime)\n            # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp for PyQtGraph (DateAxisItem expects timestamps but displays as dates)\n            unix_start = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_start)\n            unix_end = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_end)\n            plot_item.setXRange(unix_start, unix_end, padding=0)\n            plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time')\n        else:\n            plot_item.setXRange(t_start, t_end, padding=0)\n            plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time', units='s')\n        plot_item.setYRange(0, 60, padding=0)\n        plot_item.setLabel('left', track_name)\n        plot_item.hideAxis('left')\n        \n        # Ensure TrackRenderingMixin is initialized\n        self.TrackRenderingMixin_on_buildUI()\n        \n        # Add track\n        self.add_track(video_datasource, name=track_name, plot_item=plot_item)\n        \n        return video_widget, root_graphics, plot_item, dock\n\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    # To demo: supply a path to a valid XDF file containing EEG/LFP/continuous apparatus streams:",

Make sure video dependencies are installed.\")\n        \n        # Create VideoTrackDatasource if not provided\n        if video_datasource is None:\n            video_datasource = VideoTrackDatasource(\n                video_intervals_df=video_intervals_df,\n                video_folder_path=video_folder_path,\n                video_df=video_df,\n                video_paths=video_paths,\n                custom_datasource_name=custom_datasource_name,\n                reference_timestamp=reference_timestamp,\n                frames_per_second=frames_per_second,\n                thumbnail_size=thumbnail_size\n            )\n        \n        # Check if datasource has any intervals\n        if video_datasource.intervals_df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"VideoTrackDatasource has no video intervals.       "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.cursor\\plans\\create_working_track_datasources_68d6acdd.plan.md",

          "startPosition": {

          },

        },
        "contents": "### Key Conversions\n\n- **Datetime to Timestamp**: Convert pandas datetime columns to float timestamps using &#96;.timestamp()&#96; or &#96;.astype('datetime64[ns]').astype(np.float64) / 1e9&#96;\n- **Duration Handling**: Parse &#96;duration_sec&#96; (can be Timedelta, float, or string) to float seconds\n- **Interval Filtering**: Filter intervals that overlap with time windows using &#96;(t_start + t_duration &gt;= new_start) &amp; (t_start &lt;= new_end)&#96;\n\n### Specific Implementations\n\n#### 1. VideoMetadataTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;video_start_datetime&#96;, &#96;video_end_datetime&#96; (or &#96;video_start_datetime&#96; + &#96;video_duration&#96;)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: &#96;VideoThumbnailDetailRenderer&#96; (if video frames available)\n- **Color**: Blue theme (matching VideoMetadataTrack)\n\n#### 2. EEGRecordingTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;recording_datetime&#96;, &#96;duration_sec&#96; (or &#96;duration_sec_check&#96;)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching EEGRecordingTrack)\n- **Color**: Green/blue theme (matching EEGRecordingTrack)\n\n#### 3. MotionRecordingTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;recording_datetime&#96;, &#96;duration_sec&#96;\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: &#96;GenericPlotDetailRenderer&#96; or &#96;PositionPlotDetailRenderer&#96; (if position data available)\n- **Color**: Orange/red theme (matching MotionRecordingTrack)\n\n#### 4. PhoLogTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;onset&#96; (or &#96;time&#96;), &#96;duration&#96; (optional, defaults to 0 for point markers)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96; (0 duration for point markers)\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (text rendering handled by track, not datasource)\n- **Color**: Purple theme (matching PhoLogTrack)\n\n#### 5. WhisperTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;onset&#96;, &#96;duration&#96;\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching WhisperTrack)\n- **Color**: Cyan/teal theme (matching WhisperTrack)",
        "signatures": {},

        "contents": "class SimpleTimelineWidget(TrackRenderingMixin, SpecificDockWidgetManipulatingMixin, QtWidgets.QWidget):\ndef __init__(self, total_start_time=0.0, total_end_time=100.0, window_duration=10.0, window_start_time=30.0, add_example_tracks=False, reference_datetime: Optional[datetime] = None, parent=None):\n\n        \n        # Reference datetime for datetime axis alignment (shared across all tracks)\n        if reference_datetime is None:\n            from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import get_earliest_reference_datetime\n            reference_datetime = get_earliest_reference_datetime([], [])\n        self.reference_datetime = reference_datetime\n        \n        self.spikes_window = SimpleTimeWindow(\n            total_start_time, total_end_time, window_duration, window_start_time\n        )\n        \n        # Initialize plots_data and plots BEFORE setupUI (needed by mixins)\n        # Initialize track rendering mixin\n        self.TrackRenderingMixin_on_init()\n        self.TrackRenderingMixin_on_setup()\n        \n        # Setup UI (this will call add_example_tracks which uses the mixins)\n        self.setupUI()\n        \n        \n    def setupUI(self):\n        \"\"\"Setup the user interface.\"\"\"\n        # Create main layout\n        self.ui.layout = QtWidgets.QVBoxLayout(self)\n        self.ui.layout.setContentsMargins(0, 0, 0, 0)\n        \n        # Create the dock area container\n        self.ui.dynamic_docked_widget_container = NestedDockAreaWidget()\n        self.ui.dynamic_docked_widget_container.setObjectName(\"dynamic_docked_widget_container\")\n        \n        # Add to layout\n        self.ui.layout.addWidget(self.ui.dynamic_docked_widget_container)\n\n        self.TrackRenderingMixin_on_buildUI()\n        \n        # Add some example tracks (only if requested)\n        if self._add_example_tracks:\n            self.add_example_tracks()\n        \n\n    def simulate_window_scroll(self, new_start_time):\n        \"\"\"Simulate scrolling the time window (for demonstration).\"\"\"",

        "contents": "def perform_process_all_streams(streams):\n    \"\"\" main function! Processes streams to build datasources, and thus tracks.\n\n    \"\"\"\n    all_streams = {}\n    all_streams_datasources = {}\n    for i, s in enumerate(streams):\n        stream_name = s['info']['name'][0]\n        stream_type = s['info']['type'][0]\n        # if ('type' in s['info']):\n        #     a_type = s['info']['type'][0]\n        #     all_streams[a_type] = s\n        timestamps = s['time_stamps'] ## get stream data\n        time_series = s['time_series'] ## get stream data\n        \n        n_channels: int = int(s['info']['channel_count'][0])\n        print(f\"Stream {i}: {stream_name}, channels: {n_channels}, samples: {len(timestamps)}\")\n\n        # Create a single interval representing the entire stream recording\n        if len(timestamps) == 0:\n            continue\n        \n        stream_start = float(timestamps[0])\n        stream_end = float(timestamps[-1])\n        stream_duration = stream_end - stream_start\n        \n        # Create interval DataFrame with proper structure\n        intervals_df = pd.DataFrame({\n            't_start': [stream_start],\n            't_duration': [stream_duration],\n            't_end': [stream_end]\n        })\n\n        # Add visualization columns\n        intervals_df['series_vertical_offset'] = (1.0) * float(i)\n        intervals_df['series_height'] = 0.9\n        \n        # Create pens and brushes\n        color = pg.mkColor('blue')\n        color.setAlphaF(0.3)\n        pen = pg.mkPen(color, width=1)\n        brush = pg.mkBrush(color)\n        intervals_df['pen'] = [pen]\n        intervals_df['brush'] = [brush]\n\n\n        all_streams[stream_name] = intervals_df\n        has_valid_intervals: bool = (intervals_df is not None) and (len(intervals_df) &gt; 0)\n\n        # Create datasource\n        if",
        "signatures": {},

Optional[SimpleTimelineWidget]:\n\n        all_file_headers = []\n        \n        for xdf_file_path in xdf_file_paths:\n            print(f\"Loading XDF file: {xdf_file_path} ...\")\n            streams, file_header = pyxdf.load_xdf(str(xdf_file_path))\n            print(f\"  Streams loaded: {[s['info']['name'][0] for s in streams]}\")\n            all_streams_by_file.append(streams)\n            all_file_headers.append(file_header)\n        \n        # Process streams from all files and merge by stream name\n        all_streams, all_streams_datasources = perform_process_all_streams_multi_xdf(streams_list=all_streams_by_file, xdf_file_paths=xdf_file_paths)\n        \n        if not all_streams:\n            print(\"No streams found.\")\n            return None\n        \n        print(f\"Found {len(all_streams)} unique stream names after merging: {[a_name for a_name in list(all_streams_datasources.keys())]}\")\n        \n        # Get active datasources\n        active_datasources_dict = {k: v for k, v in all_streams_datasources.items() if v is not None}\n        active_datasource_list = list(active_datasources_dict.values())\n        print(f\"\\tbuild active_datasources: {len(active_datasource_list)} datasources.\")\n        \n        if not active_datasource_list:\n            print(\"No valid datasources found.\")\n            return None\n        \n        # Extract reference datetime from XDF headers (for datetime axis alignment)\n        reference_datetime = get_earliest_reference_datetime(all_file_headers, active_datasource_list)\n        if reference_datetime is not None:\n            print(f\"Using reference datetime: {reference_datetime}\")\n        else:\n            print(\"Warning: No reference datetime found, using Unix epoch\")\n        \n        # Generate window title if not provided\n        if",

// ... existing code ...
from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import float_to_datetime, datetime_to_unix_timestamp, datetime_to_float, get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header
// ... existing code ...

def perform_process_all_streams_multi_xdf(streams_list: List[List], xdf_file_paths: List[Path], file_headers: Optional[List[dict]] = None) -> Tuple[Dict, Dict]:
    """Process streams from multiple XDF files and merge streams with the same name.
    
    Streams with the same name across different files will be merged into a single datasource.
    Timestamps are converted to use a common reference datetime (earliest file's reference).
    
    Args:
        streams_list: List of stream lists (one per XDF file), where each stream list contains
                     stream dictionaries from pyxdf
        xdf_file_paths: List of Path objects corresponding to each stream list
        file_headers: Optional list of XDF file header dictionaries (one per file)
    
    Returns:
        Tuple of (all_streams dict, all_streams_datasources dict) where:
        - all_streams: Dictionary mapping stream names to merged interval DataFrames
        - all_streams_datasources: Dictionary mapping stream names to merged TrackDatasource instances
    """
    if len(streams_list) != len(xdf_file_paths):
        raise ValueError(f"streams_list length ({len(streams_list)}) must match xdf_file_paths length ({len(xdf_file_paths)})")
    
    # Extract reference datetimes from file headers
    file_reference_datetimes = {}
    if file_headers is not None:
        for i, (file_header, file_path) in enumerate(zip(file_headers, xdf_file_paths)):
            ref_dt = get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header)
            if ref_dt is not None:
                file_reference_datetimes[file_path] = ref_dt
    
    # Find earliest reference datetime (common reference for all timestamps)
    earliest_reference_datetime = None
    if file_reference_datetimes:
        earliest_reference_datetime = min(file_reference_datetimes.values())
        print(f"Using earliest reference datetime: {earliest_reference_datetime} for timestamp normalization")
    
    # Group streams by name across all files
    streams_by_file = list(zip(streams_list, xdf_file_paths))
    streams_by_name = merge_streams_by_name(streams_by_file)
    
    all_streams = {}
    all_streams_datasources = {}
    
    # Process each unique stream name
    for stream_name, stream_file_pairs in streams_by_name.items():
        print(f"\nProcessing stream '{stream_name}' from {len(stream_file_pairs)} file(s)...")
        
        # Collect intervals and detailed data from all files for this stream name
        all_intervals_dfs = []
        all_detailed_dfs = []
        stream_type = None
        stream_info = None
        
        for stream, file_path in stream_file_pairs:
            current_stream_type = stream['info']['type'][0]
            if stream_type is None:
                stream_type = current_stream_type
                stream_info = stream['info']
            elif stream_type != current_stream_type:
                print(f"WARN: Stream '{stream_name}' has different types across files: {stream_type} vs {current_stream_type}")
            
            timestamps = stream['time_stamps']
            time_series = stream['time_series']
            
            if len(timestamps) == 0:
                print(f"  Skipping empty stream from {file_path.name}")
                continue
            
            # Convert timestamps: from relative (to file's reference) to absolute, then to relative (to earliest reference)
            file_ref_dt = file_reference_datetimes.get(file_path)
            if file_ref_dt is not None and earliest_reference_datetime is not None:
                # Convert relative timestamps to absolute datetimes using file's reference
                timestamps_absolute = [float_to_datetime(float(ts), file_ref_dt) for ts in timestamps]
                # Convert absolute datetimes back to relative timestamps using earliest reference
                timestamps = np.array([datetime_to_float(dt, earliest_reference_datetime) for dt in timestamps_absolute])
            else:
                # Fallback: use timestamps as-is (relative to file start, which may cause misalignment)
                timestamps = np.array([float(ts) for ts in timestamps])
                if file_ref_dt is None:
                    print(f"  WARN: No reference datetime found for {file_path.name}, timestamps may be misaligned")
            
            stream_start = float(timestamps[0])
            stream_end = float(timestamps[-1])
            stream_duration = stream_end - stream_start
            
            # Create interval DataFrame
            intervals_df = pd.DataFrame({
                't_start': [stream_start],
                't_duration': [stream_duration],
                't_end': [stream_end]
            })
            
            # Add visualization columns
            intervals_df['series_vertical_offset'] = 0.0
            intervals_df['series_height'] = 0.9
            
            # Create pens and brushes
            color = pg.mkColor('blue')
            color.setAlphaF(0.3)
            pen = pg.mkPen(color, width=1)
            brush = pg.mkBrush(color)
            intervals_df['pen'] = [pen]
            intervals_df['brush'] = [brush]
            
            all_intervals_dfs.append(intervals_df)
            
            # Create detailed DataFrame if time_series exists
            if time_series is not None and len(time_series) > 0:
                n_channels = int(stream['info']['channel_count'][0])
                n_t_stamps, n_columns = np.shape(time_series)
                
                if n_channels == n_columns and len(timestamps) == n_t_stamps:
                    # Determine channel names based on stream type
                    if (stream_type.upper() in ['SIGNAL', 'RAW']) and ('Motion' in stream_name):
                        channel_names = modality_channels_dict['MOTION']
                    elif (stream_type.upper() in ['RAW']) and (' eQuality' in stream_name):
                        channel_names = modality_channels_dict['EEG']
                    elif (stream_type.upper() == 'EEG'):
                        channel_names = modality_channels_dict['EEG']
                    elif (stream_type.upper() in ['MARKERS']) and (stream_name in ['EventBoard', 'TextLogger']):
                        channel_names = modality_channels_dict['LOG']
                    else:
                        # Generic: use column indices
                        channel_names = [f'Channel_{i}' for i in range(n_columns)]
                    
                    time_series_df = pd.DataFrame(time_series, columns=channel_names)
                    time_series_df['t'] = timestamps
                    all_detailed_dfs.append(time_series_df)
        
        # ... rest of the function remains the same ...

        "contents": "def video_metadata_to_intervals_df(video_df: pd.DataFrame, reference_timestamp: Optional[float] = None) -&gt; pd.DataFrame:\n\"\"\"\n    if video_df.empty:\n        return pd.DataFrame()\n    \n    # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp (float seconds)\n    if 'video_start_datetime' not in video_df.columns:\n        return pd.DataFrame()\n    \n    # Convert datetime to timestamp\n    timestamps = video_df['video_start_datetime'].values.astype('datetime64[ns]').astype(np.float64) / 1e9\n    \n    # Calculate t_start relative to reference or first video\n    if reference_timestamp is None:\n        reference_timestamp = float(timestamps[0])\n    \n    t_start_values = timestamps - reference_timestamp\n    \n    # Create intervals_df\n    intervals_df = pd.DataFrame({\n        't_start': t_start_values,\n        't_duration': video_df['video_duration'].values if 'video_duration' in video_df.columns else 0.0,\n        'video_file_path': video_df['video_file_path'].values if 'video_file_path' in video_df.columns else '',\n    })\n    \n    # Preserve all other metadata columns\n    metadata_cols = [col for col in video_df.columns if col not in ['video_start_datetime', 'video_end_datetime', 'video_duration', 'video_file_path']]\n    for col in metadata_cols:\n        intervals_df[col] = video_df[col].values\n    \n    return intervals_df\n\n\n# ==================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================================== #",

        "contents": "- **Maintain backward compatibility**: Internal calculations can still use float seconds (relative to reference), but display uses datetime\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### PyQtGraph DateAxisItem Usage\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nfrom pyphoplacecellanalysis.External.pyqtgraph import DateAxisItem\naxis = DateAxisItem(orientation='bottom')\nplot_item.setAxisItems({'bottom': axis})\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Time Conversion Pattern (Ensuring Alignment)\n\n- Store reference datetime as &#96;datetime.datetime&#96; object (single instance shared across all tracks)\n- Convert float timestamps to datetime: &#96;reference_datetime + timedelta(seconds=timestamp)&#96;\n- **All tracks use the same reference_datetime** - this ensures real-time alignment\n- Use datetime values for axis ranges and labels (display layer)\n- Keep internal calculations in float seconds for performance (data layer)\n- When tracks start at different times, their timestamps are converted using the same reference, so they appear at correct absolute positions on the datetime axis\n\n### XDF Header Parsing\n\n- Check &#96;file_header&#96; for recording start time\n- Common locations: &#96;file_header['info']['recording']['start_time']&#96; or similar\n- Parse ISO format or Unix timestamp format as needed",

        "contents": "def get_earliest_reference_datetime(file_headers: list, datasources: list) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n    \"\"\"Get the earliest reference datetime from multiple sources.\n    \n    This function tries to extract reference datetimes from XDF file headers,\n    and falls back to using the earliest timestamp from datasources if headers\n    don't contain recording start times.\n    \n    Args:\n        file_headers: List of XDF file header dictionaries\n        datasources: List of TrackDatasource instances (for fallback)\n        \n    Returns:\n        datetime object, or None if no reference can be determined\n    \"\"\"\n    reference_datetimes = []\n    \n    # Try to get from XDF headers\n    for file_header in file_headers:\n        ref_dt = get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header)\n        if ref_dt is not None:\n            reference_datetimes.append(ref_dt)\n    \n    # If we found any from headers, use the earliest one\n    if reference_datetimes:\n        return min(reference_datetimes)\n    \n    # Fallback: use earliest timestamp from datasources as Unix epoch reference\n    # This assumes timestamps are relative to Unix epoch\n    if datasources:\n        earliest_timestamp = min([ds.total_df_start_end_times[0] for ds in datasources if ds is not None])\n        # Use Unix epoch (1970-01-01 UTC) as reference (timezone-aware)\n        return UNIX_EPOCH_UTC\n    \n    # Last resort: return Unix epoch (timezone-aware, UTC)\n    logger.warning(\"No reference datetime found, using Unix epoch (1970-01-01 UTC)\")\n    return UNIX_EPOCH_UTC",
        "signatures": {},

            "text": "def get_earliest_reference_datetime(file_headers: list, datasources: list) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:",

          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Get the earliest reference datetime from multiple sources.",

          },

            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 205
          },
          {
            "text": "    This function tries to extract reference datetimes from XDF file headers,",
            "lineNumber": 206
          },
          {
            "text": "    and falls back to using the earliest timestamp from datasources if headers",
            "lineNumber": 207
          },
          {
            "text": "    don't contain recording start times.",
            "lineNumber": 208
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 209
          },
          {
            "text": "    Args:",
            "lineNumber": 210
          },
          {
            "text": "        file_headers: List of XDF file header dictionaries",

          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 213
          },
          {
            "text": "    Returns:",
            "lineNumber": 214
          },
          {

            "lineNumber": 215
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 216
          },
          {
            "text": "    reference_datetimes = []",
            "lineNumber": 217
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 218
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Try to get from XDF headers",
            "lineNumber": 219
          },
          {
            "text": "    for file_header in file_headers:",
            "lineNumber": 220
          },
          {
            "text": "        ref_dt = get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header)",
            "lineNumber": 221
          },
          {

          },

          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 224
          },
          {
            "text": "    # If we found any from headers, use the earliest one",
            "lineNumber": 225
          },
          {
            "text": "    if reference_datetimes:",
            "lineNumber": 226
          },
          {
            "text": "        return min(reference_datetimes)",

          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 228
          },
          {
            "text": "    # Fallback: use earliest timestamp from datasources as Unix epoch reference",

          },

          },

          },

          },

          },

          },

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.cursor\\plans\\convert_time_axes_to_datetime_1b6dd680.plan.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {},
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 35
          }
        },
        "contents": "---\nname: Convert time axes to datetime\noverview: Convert all x (time) axes for timeline tracks from float seconds to datetime axes, using XDF file header recording start time as the reference datetime when available.\ntodos: []\n---\n\n# Convert Time Axes to Datetime Axes\n\n## Overview\n\nConvert all x (time) axes for timeline tracks from float seconds to datetime axes. **Critical requirement**: All tracks must use a single, consistent absolute datetime reference to maintain real-time alignment, even when tracks start/stop independently. The reference datetime will be extracted from XDF file headers when available, with fallback options for other data sources.\n\n## Key Changes\n\n### 1. Add Datetime Reference Management (CRITICAL: Single Reference for Alignment)\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/timeline_builder.py&#96;\n  - Extract reference datetime from XDF file headers in &#96;build_from_xdf_files()&#96;\n  - **Use a single reference datetime for the entire timeline** - this ensures all tracks align in real time\n  - For multiple XDF files: Use the earliest recording start time as the common reference\n  - Store reference datetime in &#96;TimelineBuilder&#96; instance and pass to &#96;SimpleTimelineWidget&#96;\n  - All tracks in a timeline must use the same reference datetime for proper alignment\n  - Handle fallback when XDF header doesn't have recording start time (use Unix epoch or earliest timestamp across all datasources)\n\n### 2. Update SimpleTimelineWidget\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/widgets/simple_timeline_widget.py&#96;\n  - Add &#96;reference_datetime&#96; parameter to &#96;__init__()&#96;\n  - Store reference datetime as instance attribute\n  - Convert time values when setting ranges and labels\n\n### 3. Configure PyQtGraph DateAxisItem\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/core/pyqtgraph_time_synchronized_widget.py&#96;\n  - Replace standard bottom axis with &#96;pg.DateAxisItem&#96; in &#96;_buildGraphics()&#96;",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "---",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.cursor\\plans\\create_working_track_datasources_68d6acdd.plan.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 85
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 129
          }
        },
        "contents": "### Key Conversions\n\n- **Datetime to Timestamp**: Convert pandas datetime columns to float timestamps using &#96;.timestamp()&#96; or &#96;.astype('datetime64[ns]').astype(np.float64) / 1e9&#96;\n- **Duration Handling**: Parse &#96;duration_sec&#96; (can be Timedelta, float, or string) to float seconds\n- **Interval Filtering**: Filter intervals that overlap with time windows using &#96;(t_start + t_duration &gt;= new_start) &amp; (t_start &lt;= new_end)&#96;\n\n### Specific Implementations\n\n#### 1. VideoMetadataTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;video_start_datetime&#96;, &#96;video_end_datetime&#96; (or &#96;video_start_datetime&#96; + &#96;video_duration&#96;)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: &#96;VideoThumbnailDetailRenderer&#96; (if video frames available)\n- **Color**: Blue theme (matching VideoMetadataTrack)\n\n#### 2. EEGRecordingTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;recording_datetime&#96;, &#96;duration_sec&#96; (or &#96;duration_sec_check&#96;)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching EEGRecordingTrack)\n- **Color**: Green/blue theme (matching EEGRecordingTrack)\n\n#### 3. MotionRecordingTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;recording_datetime&#96;, &#96;duration_sec&#96;\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: &#96;GenericPlotDetailRenderer&#96; or &#96;PositionPlotDetailRenderer&#96; (if position data available)\n- **Color**: Orange/red theme (matching MotionRecordingTrack)\n\n#### 4. PhoLogTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;onset&#96; (or &#96;time&#96;), &#96;duration&#96; (optional, defaults to 0 for point markers)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96; (0 duration for point markers)\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (text rendering handled by track, not datasource)\n- **Color**: Purple theme (matching PhoLogTrack)\n\n#### 5. WhisperTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;onset&#96;, &#96;duration&#96;\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching WhisperTrack)\n- **Color**: Cyan/teal theme (matching WhisperTrack)",

      "codeBlock": {

          "endPosition": {
            "line": 143

        },
        "contents": "def get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header: dict) -&gt; Optional[datetime]:\n\n    \n    for path in possible_paths:\n        try:\n            value = file_header\n            for key in path:\n                if isinstance(value, dict) and key in value:\n                    value = value[key]\n                    # XDF headers often have values as lists with single element\n                    if isinstance(value, list) and len(value) &gt; 0:\n                        value = value[0]\n                else:\n                    value = None\n                    break\n            \n            if value is not None:\n                # Try to parse as datetime\n                if isinstance(value, (int, float)):\n                    # Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch)\n                    try:\n                        dt = datetime.fromtimestamp(value, tz=timezone.utc)\n                        return dt\n                    except (ValueError, OSError):\n                        # Try as milliseconds if seconds fails\n                        try:\n                            dt = datetime.fromtimestamp(value / 1000.0, tz=timezone.utc)\n                            return dt\n                        except (ValueError, OSError):\n                            logger.warning(f\"Could not parse timestamp value {value} as Unix timestamp\")\n                            continue\n                elif isinstance(value, str):\n                    # Try ISO format or other string formats\n                    try",

          }
        },
        "contents": "def datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt: datetime) -&gt; float:\n    \"\"\"Convert datetime to Unix timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01 UTC).\n    \n    This function safely handles both naive and timezone-aware datetimes.\n    \n    Args:\n        dt: datetime object (naive or timezone-aware)\n        \n    Returns:\n        Unix timestamp as float (seconds since epoch)\n    \"\"\"\n    # If naive, assume UTC\n    if dt.tzinfo is None:\n        dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)\n    \n    return dt.timestamp()\n\n\ndef datetime_to_float(dt: datetime, reference_datetime: datetime) -&gt; float:\n    ",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "def datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt: datetime) -&gt; float:",

            "isSignature": true
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"Convert datetime to Unix timestamp (seconds since 1970-01-01 UTC).",
            "lineNumber": 170
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "text": "    This function safely handles both naive and timezone-aware datetimes.",
            "lineNumber": 172
          },
          {
            "text": "    ",
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "text": "    Args:",
            "lineNumber": 174
          },
          {
            "text": "        dt: datetime object (naive or timezone-aware)",
            "lineNumber": 175
          },
          {
            "text": "        ",
            "lineNumber": 176
          },
          {
            "text": "    Returns:",
            "lineNumber": 177
          },
          {
            "text": "        Unix timestamp as float (seconds since epoch)",
            "lineNumber": 178
          },
          {
            "text": "    \"\"\"",
            "lineNumber": 179
          },
          {
            "text": "    # If naive, assume UTC",
            "lineNumber": 180
          },
          {
            "text": "    if dt.tzinfo is None:",
            "lineNumber": 181
          },
          {
            "text": "        dt = dt.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)",

          },
          {
            "text": "    ",

          {
            "text": "    return dt.timestamp()",

          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186
          },
          {
            "text": "def datetime_to_float(dt: datetime, reference_datetime: datetime) -&gt; float:",

        "contents": "- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/core/pyqtgraph_time_synchronized_widget.py&#96;\n  - Replace standard bottom axis with &#96;pg.DateAxisItem&#96; in &#96;_buildGraphics()&#96;\n  - Update &#96;on_window_changed()&#96; to convert float times to datetime before setting ranges\n  - Ensure all time operations use datetime-aware values\n\n### 4. Update Timeline Builder Track Creation\n\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/timeline_builder.py&#96;\n  - In &#96;_add_tracks_to_timeline()&#96;, configure axes with datetime formatting\n  - Convert &#96;setXRange()&#96; calls to use datetime values\n  - Update &#96;setLabel()&#96; to use datetime format instead of 's' units\n\n### 5. Update Other Axis Configuration Points\n\n- **Files to update**:\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/widgets/simple_timeline_widget.py&#96; - &#96;add_video_track()&#96; method\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/__main__.py&#96; - Main entry point axis configuration\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/docking/specific_dock_widget_mixin.py&#96; - Overview plot axes\n  - &#96;pypho_timeline/docking/dynamic_dock_display_area.py&#96; - Widget axis configuration\n\n### 6. Helper Functions\n\n- **New file**: &#96;pypho_timeline/utils/datetime_helpers.py&#96;\n  - &#96;get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header)&#96; - Extract datetime from XDF header\n  - &#96;float_to_datetime(timestamp, reference_datetime)&#96; - Convert float to datetime\n  - &#96;datetime_to_float(dt, reference_datetime)&#96; - Convert datetime back to float (for internal calculations)\n\n### 7. Handle Edge Cases (Maintaining Real-Time Alignment)\n\n- **Multiple XDF files**: Use the earliest reference datetime across all files as the common reference point\n- **Mixed data sources**: When combining XDF and video tracks, use the earliest available reference datetime\n- **Video-only timelines**: Use Unix epoch or configurable reference (must be consistent across all video tracks)\n- **Missing XDF header info**: Fallback to Unix epoch (1970-01-01) or use earliest timestamp across all datasources\n- **Track start/stop independence**: Tracks can have different start/stop times, but all use the same reference datetime for alignment\n- **Maintain backward compatibility**: Internal calculations can still use float seconds (relative to reference), but display uses datetime\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### PyQtGraph DateAxisItem Usage\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python",

        "contents": "- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching WhisperTrack)\n- **Color**: Cyan/teal theme (matching WhisperTrack)\n\n#### 6. XDFStreamTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;recording_datetime&#96; (or &#96;first_timestamp_dt&#96;), &#96;duration_sec&#96; (or &#96;last_timestamp_dt&#96;)\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (overview only, matching XDFStreamTrack)\n- **Color**: Gray theme (matching XDFStreamTrack)\n\n#### 7. StringDataTrackDatasource\n\n- **Input**: DataFrame with &#96;onset&#96;, &#96;duration&#96;\n- **Output**: Intervals with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;\n- **Detail Renderer**: None (base class, overview only)\n- **Color**: Default (subclasses set their own)\n\n### Required Methods Implementation\n\nEach datasource must implement:\n\n1. **&#96;df&#96; property**: Returns DataFrame with &#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;, and visualization columns\n2. **&#96;time_column_names&#96; property**: Returns &#96;['t_start', 't_duration', 't_end']&#96;\n3. **&#96;total_df_start_end_times&#96; property**: Returns &#96;(min_t_start, max_t_end)&#96; tuple\n4. **&#96;get_updated_data_window(new_start, new_end)&#96;**: Returns overlapping intervals\n5. **&#96;fetch_detailed_data(interval)&#96;**: Returns detailed data (DataFrame, dict, or None)\n6. **&#96;get_detail_renderer()&#96;**: Returns &#96;DetailRenderer&#96; instance or None\n7. **&#96;update_visualization_properties(function)&#96;**: Updates visualization columns\n\n### Helper Utilities\n\nCreate helper functions for:\n\n- Parsing duration to seconds (handle Timedelta, float, string)\n- Converting datetime to timestamp\n- Normalizing datetime columns to UTC-naive\n- Creating default pens/brushes with colors and alpha\n\n### Testing Considerations\n\nEach datasource should:\n\n- Handle empty DataFrames gracefully\n- Handle missing columns with sensible defaults\n- Convert datetime columns correctly (timezone-aware and naive)\n- Filter intervals correctly for time windows\n- Generate unique cache keys for intervals\n\n## Files to Create\n\n- &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/modality_datasources.py&#96; - All modality datasources\n\n## Files to Update\n\n- &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/__init__.py&#96; - Export new datasources",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\pypho_timeline\\timeline_builder.py",
        "range": {

        "contents": "class TimelineBuilder:\ndef _add_tracks_to_timeline(self, timeline: SimpleTimelineWidget, datasources: List[TrackDatasource]) -&gt; None:\n\n            \n            # Set the plot to show the full time range (convert to datetime then Unix timestamp if reference available)\n            if timeline.reference_datetime is not None:\n                from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import float_to_datetime, datetime_to_unix_timestamp\n                dt_start = float_to_datetime(timeline.total_data_start_time, timeline.reference_datetime)\n                dt_end = float_to_datetime(timeline.total_data_end_time, timeline.reference_datetime)\n                # Convert datetime to Unix timestamp for PyQtGraph (DateAxisItem expects timestamps but displays as dates)\n                unix_start = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_start)\n                unix_end = datetime_to_unix_timestamp(dt_end)\n                a_plot_item.setXRange(unix_start, unix_end, padding=0)\n                a_plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time')\n            else:\n                a_plot_item.setXRange(timeline.total_data_start_time, timeline.total_data_end_time, padding=0)\n                a_plot_item.setLabel('bottom', 'Time', units='s')\n            a_plot_item.setYRange(0, 1, padding=0)\n            a_plot_item.setLabel('left', datasource.custom_datasource_name)\n            a_plot_item.hideAxis('left')  # Hide Y-axis for cleaner look\n            \n            # Add the track to the plot\n            a_track_name: str = datasource.custom_datasource_name\n            timeline.add_track(datasource, name=a_track_name, plot_item=a_plot_item)\n        ## END for datasource in datasources...\n        \n        # Hide x-axis labels for all tracks except the bottom-most one",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.cursor\\plans\\create_working_track_datasources_68d6acdd.plan.md",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 42
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 90
          }
        },
        "contents": "    status: completed\n    dependencies:\n      - implement_helper_utilities\n  - id: update_init_exports\n    content: Update __init__.py to export all new datasources\n    status: completed\n    dependencies:\n      - create_modality_datasources_file\n---\n\n# Create Working Track Datasources for pyPhoTimeline\n\n## Overview\n\nCreate working datasource implementations for &#96;pyPhoTimeline&#96; that derive from &#96;BaseTrackDatasource&#96;. These will replace the non-working implementations in &#96;PhoOfflineEEGAnalysis&#96; timeline tracks. Each datasource will convert from the timeline track data formats (datetime-based) to the &#96;BaseTrackDatasource&#96; interface (float timestamp-based).\n\n## Modalities to Implement\n\nBased on the timeline tracks, create datasources for:\n\n1. **VideoMetadataTrackDatasource** - Video recordings\n2. **EEGRecordingTrackDatasource** - EEG recordings  \n3. **MotionRecordingTrackDatasource** - Motion recordings\n4. **PhoLogTrackDatasource** - PHO_LOG annotations\n5. **WhisperTrackDatasource** - Whisper transcripts\n6. **XDFStreamTrackDatasource** - Generic XDF streams\n7. **StringDataTrackDatasource** - Generic string/comment data (base class)\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### File Structure\n\nCreate a new file: &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/modality_datasources.py&#96;This file will contain all modality-specific datasources.\n\n### Common Pattern\n\nEach datasource will:\n\n1. Accept data in the format expected by the corresponding timeline track (DataFrame with datetime columns)\n2. Convert datetime columns to float timestamps (&#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;)\n3. Create visualization columns (&#96;series_vertical_offset&#96;, &#96;series_height&#96;, &#96;pen&#96;, &#96;brush&#96;)\n4. Implement all required &#96;BaseTrackDatasource&#96; abstract methods\n5. Provide appropriate detail renderers (or None for tracks without detail views)\n\n### Key Conversions\n\n- **Datetime to Timestamp**: Convert pandas datetime columns to float timestamps using &#96;.timestamp()&#96; or &#96;.astype('datetime64[ns]').astype(np.float64) / 1e9&#96;\n- **Duration Handling**: Parse &#96;duration_sec&#96; (can be Timedelta, float, or string) to float seconds",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "    status: completed",
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "text": "    dependencies:",
            "lineNumber": 44
          },

            "text": "      - implement_helper_utilities",
            "lineNumber": 45
