```json
          },
          {
            "text": "# Create Working Track Datasources for pyPhoTimeline",
            "lineNumber": 53

          {
            "lineNumber": 54
          },
          {
            "text": "## Overview",
            "lineNumber": 55
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56
          },
          {
            "text": "Create working datasource implementations for &#96;pyPhoTimeline&#96; that derive from &#96;BaseTrackDatasource&#96;. These will replace the non-working implementations in &#96;PhoOfflineEEGAnalysis&#96; timeline tracks. Each datasource will convert from the timeline track data formats (datetime-based) to the &#96;BaseTrackDatasource&#96; interface (float timestamp-based).",
            "lineNumber": 57
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58
          },
          {
            "text": "## Modalities to Implement",
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60
          },
          {
            "text": "Based on the timeline tracks, create datasources for:",
            "lineNumber": 61
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62
          },
          {
            "text": "1. **VideoMetadataTrackDatasource** - Video recordings",
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "text": "2. **EEGRecordingTrackDatasource** - EEG recordings  ",
            "lineNumber": 64
          },
          {
            "text": "3. **MotionRecordingTrackDatasource** - Motion recordings",
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "text": "4. **PhoLogTrackDatasource** - PHO_LOG annotations",
            "lineNumber": 66
          },
          {
            "text": "5. **WhisperTrackDatasource** - Whisper transcripts",
            "lineNumber": 67
          },
          {
            "text": "6. **XDFStreamTrackDatasource** - Generic XDF streams",
            "lineNumber": 68
          },
          {
            "text": "7. **StringDataTrackDatasource** - Generic string/comment data (base class)",
            "lineNumber": 69
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 70
          },
          {
            "text": "## Implementation Details",
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72
          },
          {
            "text": "### File Structure",
            "lineNumber": 73
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 74
          },
          {
            "text": "Create a new file: &#96;pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/modality_datasources.py&#96;This file will contain all modality-specific datasources.",
            "lineNumber": 75
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 76
          },
          {
            "text": "### Common Pattern",
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78
          },
          {
            "text": "Each datasource will:",
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80
          },
          {
            "text": "1. Accept data in the format expected by the corresponding timeline track (DataFrame with datetime columns)",
            "lineNumber": 81
          },
          {
            "text": "2. Convert datetime columns to float timestamps (&#96;t_start&#96;, &#96;t_duration&#96;)",
            "lineNumber": 82
          },
          {
            "text": "3. Create visualization columns (&#96;series_vertical_offset&#96;, &#96;series_height&#96;, &#96;pen&#96;, &#96;brush&#96;)",
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "text": "4. Implement all required &#96;BaseTrackDatasource&#96; abstract methods",
            "lineNumber": 84
          },
          {
            "text": "5. Provide appropriate detail renderers (or None for tracks without detail views)",
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86
          },
          {
            "text": "### Key Conversions",
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Datetime to Timestamp**: Convert pandas datetime columns to float timestamps using &#96;.timestamp()&#96; or &#96;.astype('datetime64[ns]').astype(np.float64) / 1e9&#96;",
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "text": "- **Duration Handling**: Parse &#96;duration_sec&#96; (can be Timedelta, float, or string) to float seconds",
            "lineNumber": 90
          }
        ]
      },
      "score": 0.29892268776893616

        "contents": "@runtime_checkable\nclass TrackDatasource(Protocol):\n    \"\"\"Protocol for track datasources that provide overview intervals and detailed data.\n    \n    Extends IntervalsDatasource to add methods for fetching detailed data asynchronously\n    and providing a renderer for the detailed view.\n    \n    **Required Methods** (must be implemented):\n        - &#96;df&#96; property: Returns the DataFrame containing interval data\n        - &#96;time_column_names&#96; property: Returns list of time-related column names\n        - &#96;total_df_start_end_times&#96; property: Returns (start, end) tuple for entire dataset\n        - &#96;get_updated_data_window(new_start, new_end)&#96;: Returns intervals in time window\n        - &#96;fetch_detailed_data(interval)&#96;: Fetches detailed data for an interval (thread-safe)\n        - &#96;get_detail_renderer()&#96;: Returns DetailRenderer instance for this track type\n    \n    **Optional Methods** (have default implementations in BaseTrackDatasource):\n        - &#96;get_overview_intervals()&#96;: Returns overview interval data (defaults to &#96;df&#96;)\n        - &#96;get_detail_cache_key(interval)&#96;: Returns unique cache key (defaults to time-based key)\n        - &#96;update_visualization_properties(function)&#96;: Updates visualization columns\n    \n    **Required Attributes**:\n        - &#96;custom_datasource_name&#96;: str - Name identifier for this datasource\n        - &#96;source_data_changed_signal&#96;: QtCore.Signal - Signal emitted when data changes\n    \"\"\"\n    \n    # Required attributes (from IntervalsDatasource)\n    custom_datasource_name: str\n    source_data_changed_signal: QtCore.Signal\n    \n    # Required properties (from IntervalsDatasource)\n    @property\n    def df(self) -&gt; Make sure video dependencies are installed.\")\n        \n        # Create VideoTrackDatasource if not provided\n        if video_datasource is None:\n            video_datasource = VideoTrackDatasource(\n                video_intervals_df=video_intervals_df,\n                video_folder_path=video_folder_path,\n                video_df=video_df,\n                video_paths=video_paths,\n                custom_datasource_name=custom_datasource_name,\n                reference_timestamp=reference_timestamp,\n                frames_per_second=frames_per_second,\n                thumbnail_size=thumbnail_size\n            )\n        \n        # Check if datasource has any intervals\n        if video_datasource.intervals_df.empty:\n            raise ValueError(\"VideoTrackDatasource has no video intervals. Check that video files exist and are valid.\")\n        \n        # Get reference datetime (use reference_timestamp if available, otherwise fallback)\n        from pypho_timeline.utils.datetime_helpers import get_earliest_reference_datetime\n        reference_datetime = None\n        if reference_timestamp is not None:\n            # Convert reference_timestamp (float) to datetime (assuming Unix epoch, UTC)\n            reference_datetime = datetime.fromtimestamp(reference_timestamp, tz=timezone.utc)\n        else:\n            # Fallback to earliest timestamp or Unix epoch\n            reference_datetime = get_earliest_reference_datetime([], [video_datasource])\n        \n        # Build timeline from the single video datasource\n        return",

        "contents": "def perform_process_all_streams(streams):\n    \"\"\" main function! Processes streams to build datasources, and thus tracks.\n\n    \"\"\"\n    all_streams = {}\n    all_streams_datasources = {}\n    for i, s in enumerate(streams):\n        stream_name = s['info']['name'][0]\n        stream_type = s['info']['type'][0]\n        # if ('type' in s['info']):\n        #     a_type = s['info']['type'][0]\n        #     all_streams[a_type] = s\n        timestamps = s['time_stamps'] ## get stream data\n        time_series = s['time_series'] ## get stream data\n        \n        n_channels: int = int(s['info']['channel_count'][0])\n        print(f\"Stream {i}: {stream_name}, channels: {n_channels}, samples: {len(timestamps)}\")\n\n        # Create a single interval representing the entire stream recording\n        if len(timestamps) == 0:\n            continue\n        \n        stream_start = float(timestamps[0])\n        stream_end = float(timestamps[-1])\n        stream_duration = stream_end - stream_start\n        \n        # Create interval DataFrame with proper structure\n        intervals_df = pd.DataFrame({\n            't_start': [stream_start],\n            't_duration': [stream_duration],\n            't_end': [stream_end]\n        })\n\n        # Add visualization columns\n        intervals_df['series_vertical_offset'] = (1.0) * float(i)\n        intervals_df['series_height'] = 0.9\n        \n        # Create pens and brushes\n        color = pg.mkColor('blue')\n        color.setAlphaF(0.3)\n        pen = pg.mkPen(color, width=1)\n        brush = pg.mkBrush(color)\n        intervals_df['pen'] = [pen]\n        intervals_df['brush'] = [brush]\n\n\n        all_streams[stream_name] = intervals_df\n        has_valid_intervals: bool = (intervals_df is not None) and (len(intervals_df) &gt; 0)\n\n        # Create datasource\n        if",

        "contents": "class BaseTrackDatasource(ABC):\n\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self):\n        \"\"\"Initialize the base datasource with common attributes.\"\"\"\n        self.custom_datasource_name = \"BaseTrackDatasource\"\n        self.source_data_changed_signal = QtCore.Signal()\n    \n    # Required abstract properties\n    @property\n    @abstractmethod\n    def df(self) -&gt; pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"The dataframe containing interval data with columns ['t_start', 't_duration', ...]\"\"\"\n        ...\n    \n    @property\n    @abstractmethod\n    def time_column_names(self) -&gt; list:\n        \"\"\"The names of time-related columns (e.g., ['t_start', 't_duration', 't_end'])\"\"\"\n        ...\n    \n    @property\n    @abstractmethod\n    def total_df_start_end_times(self) -&gt; Tuple[float, float]:\n        \"\"\"Returns (earliest_time, latest_time) for the entire dataset\"\"\"\n        ...\n    \n    # Required abstract methods\n    @abstractmethod\n    def get_updated_data_window(self, new_start: float, new_end: float) -&gt; pd.DataFrame:\n        \"\"\"Returns the subset of intervals that overlap with the given time window\"\"\"\n        ...\n    \n    @abstractmethod\n    def fetch_detailed_data(self, interval: pd.Series) -&gt; Any:\n        \"\"\"Fetch detailed data for a specific interval (synchronous, called from worker thread).\n        \n        This method will be called from a worker thread, so it should be thread-safe.\n        It should not perform any GUI operations.\n        \n        Args:\n            interval: Series with at least 't_start' and 't_duration' columns\n            \n        Returns:\n            Detailed data for this interval (type depends on track type, e.g., DataFrame for position,\n            image array for video, etc.)\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n    \n    @abstractmethod\n    def get_detail_renderer(self) -&gt; DetailRenderer:\n        \"\"\"Get the renderer for detailed views of this track type.\n        \n        Returns:\n            A DetailRenderer instance that knows how to render the detailed data\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n    \n    # Default implementations for optional methods\n    def get_overview_intervals(self) -&gt; list:\n            \"\"\"The names of time-related columns (e.g., ['t_start', 't_duration', 't_end'])\"\"\"\n            ...\n        \n        @property\n        def total_df_start_end_times(self) -&gt;         "contents": "class InstancedEpochQuadVisual(Visual):\n\n    \n    def set_epochs(self, epochs_data: List[Dict[str, Any]], reference_datetime: Optional[datetime] = None):\n        \"\"\"Update epoch rectangles from data.\n        \n        Args:\n            epochs_data: List of dicts with keys: 't_start', 't_duration', 'series_vertical_offset',\n                        'series_height', 'pen', 'brush' (or 'color')\n            reference_datetime: Optional reference datetime for time conversion\n        \"\"\"\n        n = len(epochs_data)\n        if n &gt; self.max_epochs:\n            logger.warning(f\"InstancedEpochQuadVisual: {n} epochs exceeds max_epochs={self.max_epochs}, truncating\")\n            epochs_data = epochs_data[:self.max_epochs]\n            n = self.max_epochs\n        \n        # Pre-allocate arrays\n        shifts = np.zeros((self.max_epochs, 2), dtype=np.float32)\n        sizes = np.zeros((self.max_epochs, 2), dtype=np.float32)\n        colors = np.zeros((self.max_epochs, 4), dtype=np.float32)\n        \n        # Convert datetime to float if needed\n        def time_to_float(t_val):\n            \"\"\"Convert time value to float.\"\"\"\n            if isinstance(t_val, datetime):\n                if reference_datetime is None:\n                    return t_val.timestamp()\n                else:\n                    return datetime_to_float(t_val, reference_datetime)\n            return float(t_val)\n        \n        # Extract color from pen/brush\n        def extract_color(pen_or_brush):\n            \"\"\"",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": ".\\.cursor\\plans\\video_track_implementation_b74ff64d.plan.md",

        "contents": "&#96;&#96;&#96;python\ndef video_metadata_to_intervals_df(video_df: pd.DataFrame, reference_timestamp: Optional[float] = None) -&gt; pd.DataFrame:\n    \"\"\"Convert VideoMetadataParser output to intervals_df format.\n    \n    Args:\n        video_df: DataFrame from VideoMetadataParser.parse_video_folder()\n        reference_timestamp: Optional reference timestamp (Unix epoch seconds). \n                           If None, uses first video's start time as t=0.\n    \n    Returns:\n        DataFrame with columns ['t_start', 't_duration', 'video_file_path', ...]\n    \"\"\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nConversion logic:\n\n- Convert &#96;video_start_datetime&#96; to Unix timestamp (float seconds)\n- Calculate &#96;t_start&#96; relative to reference or first video\n- Set &#96;t_duration&#96; from &#96;video_duration&#96; column\n- Preserve metadata columns (video_file_path, fps, resolution, etc.)\n\n### 4. Update VideoTrackDatasource\n\n**File**: &#96;pyPhoTimeline/pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/specific/video.py&#96;Enhance &#96;VideoTrackDatasource.__init__()&#96; to accept:\n\n- &#96;video_intervals_df&#96;: DataFrame (existing behavior)\n- &#96;video_folder_path&#96;: Path to folder (new - will parse using VideoMetadataParser)\n- &#96;video_df&#96;: Pre-parsed DataFrame from VideoMetadataParser (new)\n\nUpdate initialization:\n\n- If &#96;video_folder_path&#96; provided, call &#96;VideoMetadataParser.parse_video_folder()&#96;\n- Convert to intervals_df using helper function\n- Store video_file_path in intervals_df for frame loading\n- Use blue color scheme (matching PhoOfflineEEGAnalysis)\n\n### 5. Implement actual video frame loading\n\n**File**: &#96;pyPhoTimeline/pypho_timeline/rendering/datasources/specific/video.py&#96;Update &#96;VideoTrackDatasource.fetch_detailed_data()&#96;:\n\n- Read &#96;video_file_path&#96; from interval Series\n- Use cv2.VideoCapture to load video file\n- Extract frames at regular intervals (e.g., 10 fps or configurable)\n- Calculate frame timestamps relative to interval start\n- Return dict with 'frames' (list of numpy arrays) and 'timestamps' (array)\n- Handle errors gracefully (return empty frames if video can't be loaded)\n\nFrame extraction logic:\n\n- Open video with cv2.VideoCapture",
```

Creating a plan to fix timestamp conversion while preserving real datetimes:


---

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "plan": "# Fix XDF Timestamp Conversion to Preserve Real Datetimes\n\n## Problem\nXDF files contain timestamps that are relative to each file's recording start time. When multiple XDF files are loaded, their timestamps are not being converted to use a common reference datetime, causing misalignment on the timeline. Each file has its own reference datetime in the header, but timestamps are being used as-is without proper conversion.\n\n## Solution\nConvert each file's relative timestamps to absolute datetimes using that file's reference datetime, then normalize all timestamps to use the earliest reference datetime as the common reference. This ensures:\n- All datasources display correct absolute datetimes\n- Timestamps are properly aligned across multiple files\n- The internal float-based system (relative to reference) is preserved\n\n## Changes Required\n\n### 1. Update imports in &#96;simple_timeline_widget.py&#96;\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/widgets/simple_timeline_widget.py&#96;\n- Add &#96;datetime_to_float&#96; and &#96;get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header&#96; to the imports from &#96;datetime_helpers&#96;\n- **Location**: Line 16\n\n### 2. Modify &#96;perform_process_all_streams_multi_xdf&#96; function\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/widgets/simple_timeline_widget.py&#96;\n- **Location**: Lines 421-610\n- **Changes**:\n  - Add &#96;file_headers: Optional[List[dict]] = None&#96; parameter to function signature\n  - Extract reference datetime from each file header before processing streams\n  - Find the earliest reference datetime across all files (this becomes the common reference)\n  - For each stream, convert relative timestamps to absolute datetimes using that file's reference\n  - Convert absolute datetimes back to relative timestamps using the earliest reference\n  - Apply converted timestamps to both &#96;intervals_df&#96; and &#96;time_series_df&#96;\n  - Add logging to show which reference datetime is being used\n\n### 3. Update call site in &#96;timeline_builder.py&#96;\n- **File**: &#96;pypho_timeline/timeline_builder.py&#96;\n- **Location**: Line 151\n- **Change**: Pass &#96;file_headers=all_file_headers&#96; to &#96;perform_process_all_streams_multi_xdf&#96;\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### Timestamp Conversion Flow\n1. For each XDF file, extract reference datetime from header using &#96;get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header()&#96;\n2. Find the earliest reference datetime across all files\n3. For each stream in each file:\n   - Get relative timestamps from stream (relative to that file's start)\n   - Convert to absolute datetimes: &#96;float_to_datetime(timestamp, file_reference_datetime)&#96;\n   - Convert back to relative timestamps: &#96;datetime_to_float(absolute_datetime, earliest_reference_datetime)&#96;\n4. Store normalized timestamps in datasources (still as floats, but now relative to common reference)\n5. Display uses &#96;earliest_reference_datetime&#96; to convert back to absolute datetimes for display\n\n### Key Code Pattern\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# Extract reference datetimes\nfile_reference_datetimes = {}\nfor file_header, file_path in zip(file_headers, xdf_file_paths):\n    ref_dt = get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header(file_header)\n    if ref_dt is not None:\n        file_reference_datetimes[file_path] = ref_dt\n\n# Find earliest reference\nearliest_reference_datetime = min(file_reference_datetimes.values())\n\n# Convert timestamps for each stream\nfile_ref_dt = file_reference_datetimes.get(file_path)\nif file_ref_dt is not None and earliest_reference_datetime is not None:\n    timestamps_absolute = [float_to_datetime(float(ts), file_ref_dt) for ts in timestamps]\n    timestamps = np.array([datetime_to_float(dt, earliest_reference_datetime) for dt in timestamps_absolute])\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Benefits\n- All datasources use the same reference datetime internally\n- Timestamps are properly aligned across multiple XDF files\n- Real absolute datetimes are preserved and displayed correctly\n- Backward compatible with existing float-based timestamp system\n- No changes needed to datasource classes or display code\n\n## Testing\nAfter implementation, verify:\n- Multiple XDF files with different recording start times align correctly\n- Timeline displays show correct absolute datetimes\n- All tracks appear at their correct absolute time positions\n- No timestamp misalignment between tracks from different files",
  "name": "Fix XDF timestamp conversion to preserve real datetimes",
  "todos": [
    {
      "content": "Update imports in simple_timeline_widget.py to include datetime_to_float and get_reference_datetime_from_xdf_header",

    },
    {
      "content": "Add file_headers parameter to perform_process_all_streams_multi_xdf function signature",
      "status": "pending",
      "id": "modify-function-signature"
    },
    {
      "content": "Extract reference datetime from each file header and find earliest reference",
      "status": "pending",
      "id": "extract-reference-datetimes"
    },
    {
      "content": "Convert each file's relative timestamps to absolute, then to relative using earliest reference",
      "status": "pending",
      "id": "convert-timestamps"
    },
    {
      "content": "Update timeline_builder.py to pass file_headers to perform_process_all_streams_multi_xdf",
      "status": "pending",
      "id": "update-call-site"
    }
  ],
  "overview": "Fix timestamp conversion in XDF file processing so that each file's relative timestamps are converted to absolute datetimes using that file's reference, then normalized to a common reference (earliest file's reference) to ensure all datasources display correct absolute datetimes on the timeline."
}
```
